**Page title:** Awards — U.S. News & World Report's Best High Schools U.S. News & World Reports - Best High Schools: recognized 2008–2024 (every year listed 2008 through 2024). US News & World Report published their high school rankings for 2024-25 on Tuesday, April 23. Once again, DBHS was ranked among the best schools in the state and the nation. The U.S. News Best High Schools rankings include data on nearly 25,000 public high schools in 50 states and the District of Columbia. Nearly 18,000 schools were ranked on six factors based on their performance on state assessments and how well they prepare students for college. **Rankings (2024-25):** - #408 in National Rankings - #56 in California High Schools - #21 in Los Angeles, CA Metro Area High Schools - #149 in STEM High Schools DBHS's rankings are a result of a partnership among students, staff, parents, community members, and the district office in supporting the balance provided by the four A's: Academics, Activities, the Arts, and Athletics. **Page title:** Wellness Center **DBHS Wellness Center — "Empowering Student Wellness from Within"** ### Mission Statement The Diamond Bar High School Wellness Staff is committed to creating a supportive environment for students by providing wellness education and resources. Peer counselors are equipped to assist students by guiding them to develop healthy strategies for personal well-being, which promotes individual academic success and improves the overall school climate. ### Vision Statement The Wellness Staff continually strives to be responsive to the overall needs of all students. They believe the physical, emotional, social, intellectual, cultural, and spiritual dimensions of students are the foundation of a well-balanced approach to personal success. The Wellness Center is an inclusive, nonjudgmental space available for all students seeking additional support and/or access to wellness services, education, and resources. **Location:** Room #254. Give me a detailed overview of Work Permits.

**Page title:** Work Permits Students under the age of eighteen (18) must have a work permit on file with the school and their employer. IT'S THE LAW! ### To apply for a work permit: 1. Once you have been hired by an employer: - Complete the Minor's Information section - Complete the School Information Section - Have the application signed by the minor's parent/legal guardian - Have the employer complete and sign the Employer Information section. 2. To turn in a completed work permit, go see **Ms. Ayala** in the Career Center, located inside the Express Library (Rm. 265). Processing a work permit can take 1–2 business days. ### Work Permit Renewals If you are under the age of 18, work permits must be renewed each year. Follow the instructions above to renew. ### Young Workers Health and Safety Information Visit http://www.youngworkers.org/ for the basics about young worker health and safety, with a downloadable fact sheet "Are You A Working Teen?" in English and Spanish. Linked documents: DBHS Work Permit (PDF); CA Child Labor Laws (https://www.dir.ca.gov/dlse/childlaborlawpamphlet.pdf).
How is Parchment related to Transcripts?

**Page title:** Transcripts Current and past students access official transcripts through Docufide by Parchment, which enables users to instantly send transcripts to institutions of choice. To register accurately through Parchment, the following is required: use ONLY ONE email address per student; register using your LEGAL NAME; current residence address; date of birth; year of graduation. Contact **Mrs. Han** if your request is not processed after 2 business days. Using Docufide enables users to request transcripts for personal use and to send to universities, scholarship foundations, and/or the NCAA Eligibility Center. **Special Note for Current Seniors:** Final transcripts are requested by the student using Parchment. Seniors must follow the steps to request their FINAL transcript to be sent to the university they will attend. Tell me about Transcripts.

**Page title:** Transcripts Current and past students access official transcripts through Docufide by Parchment, which enables users to instantly send transcripts to institutions of choice. To register accurately through Parchment, the following is required: use ONLY ONE email address per student; register using your LEGAL NAME; current residence address; date of birth; year of graduation. Contact **Mrs. Han** if your request is not processed after 2 business days. Using Docufide enables users to request transcripts for personal use and to send to universities, scholarship foundations, and/or the NCAA Eligibility Center. **Special Note for Current Seniors:** Final transcripts are requested by the student using Parchment. Seniors must follow the steps to request their FINAL transcript to be sent to the university they will attend. This is not done directly by DBHS or the GLC — it will only be sent if the student has requested the final transcript through Parchment. - Register & order transcripts: www.parchment.com - Direct link: http://www.parchment.com/c/college/high-school-2725-Diamond-Bar-High-School.html ---

How is physical related to Vision, Mission & SLOs?

**Page title:** Vision, Mission & SLOs ### Vision Statement Diamond Bar High School will be a premier school committed to the intellectual, physical, and social development of every student. Diamond Bar will be a community where every student's cultural heritage enriches the learning experience for all students. ### Mission Diamond Bar will value the five "A's" of Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics so that each student will experience a personalized educational program selected from a rigorous, rich and varied curriculum. Students will be challenged to discover their unique gifts and talents; staff members will support every student in realizing his or her potential. Working in partnership with parents and the community, the Diamond Bar High School staff will provide learning opportunities that reach beyond the boundaries of the classroom and ensure every student a chance for success in college and career, and as citizens and leaders of the 21st century. What does the DBHS knowledge base say about Student Fees?

**Page title:** Student Fees (Fee Info) The California Constitution mandates that public education be provided to students free of charge, unless a charge is specifically authorized by law for a particular program or activity. This constitutional right of free access encompasses all educational activities, whether curricular or extracurricular, and regardless of whether credit is awarded for the educational activity. The right of free access also prohibits mandated purchases of materials, supplies, equipment or uniforms associated with the activity, as well as the payment of security deposits for access, participation, materials or equipment. A process that allows for a waiver process for an otherwise mandatory fee, charge or deposit does not render it constitutionally permissible. Further information (district): http://www.wvusd.k12.ca.us/apps/pages/index.jsp?uREC_ID=298183&type=d

How is school related to Awards & Recognition?

**Page title:** Awards — U.S. News & World Report's Best High Schools U.S. News & World Reports - Best High Schools: recognized 2008–2024 (every year listed 2008 through 2024). US News & World Report published their high school rankings for 2024-25 on Tuesday, April 23. Once again, DBHS was ranked among the best schools in the state and the nation. The U.S. News Best High Schools rankings include data on nearly 25,000 public high schools in 50 states and the District of Columbia. Nearly 18,000 schools were ranked on six factors based on their performance on state assessments and how well they prepare students for college. **Rankings (2024-25):** - #408 in National Rankings - #56 in California High Schools - #21 in Los Angeles, CA Metro Area High Schools - #149 in STEM High Schools DBHS's rankings are a result of a partnership among students, staff, parents, community members, and the district office in supporting the balance provided by the four A's: Academics, Activities, the Arts, and Athletics. At the center of these foundations is the belief in providing equitable access to opportunities for success to ALL students. **The six ranking factors:** | Factor | Weight | Description |

What information is available regarding Work Permits?

**Page title:** Work Permits Students under the age of eighteen (18) must have a work permit on file with the school and their employer. IT'S THE LAW! ### To apply for a work permit: 1. Once you have been hired by an employer: - Complete the Minor's Information section - Complete the School Information Section - Have the application signed by the minor's parent/legal guardian - Have the employer complete and sign the Employer Information section. 2. To turn in a completed work permit, go see **Ms. Ayala** in the Career Center, located inside the Express Library (Rm. 265). Processing a work permit can take 1–2 business days. ### Work Permit Renewals If you are under the age of 18, work permits must be renewed each year. Follow the instructions above to renew. ### Young Workers Health and Safety Information Visit http://www.youngworkers.org/ for the basics about young worker health and safety, with a downloadable fact sheet "Are You A Working Teen?" in English and Spanish. Linked documents: DBHS Work Permit (PDF); CA Child Labor Laws (https://www.dir.ca.gov/dlse/childlaborlawpamphlet.pdf).
Give me a detailed overview of Mathematics Department.

**Page title:** DBHS Mathematics! Welcome to Brahma Mathematics. - 2011, 2013, 2014, 2016, 2022 Regional Champions for the California Mathematics League - 2005 and 2007 AP Calculus World Champions - Winner of the prestigious 2007-2008 Siemens Award for Advanced Placement Any student who wants a recommendation letter from a math teacher for a summer off-campus opportunity or scholarship must submit a request to that teacher no later than THREE WEEKS before the application due date. Each math teacher has their own requirements; check at least FIVE WEEKS prior to the due date. Math section sub-pages: DBHS Mathematics!; Math Help!; Math Pathways; Math Team; Math Team Files; California Mathematics League; Math Madness!; American Mathematics Competition; Are you ready for Algebra II / Trigonometry Honors?

What services are provided by Advanced Placement (AP)?

**Page title:** AP Program – Home AP Coordinator: **Jennifer Burns**. This page focuses on AP exam registration and payment logistics (it does not enumerate the individual AP courses offered). ### AP Updates - Log in to your AP Classroom if you have not already (your teacher provides a join code). If your class period changed and you are in the wrong AP classroom, email Jennifer Burns. - Use your personal email address and legal name when registering. If you used your school email or a nickname, contact College Board to change it. College Board phone: (888) 225-5427, M–F 6am–3pm. - Once registered in AP Classroom, pay for your exam through the DBHS Webstore. (Do not pay for an exam for a class you are not enrolled in.) ### Important AP Dates (as published) - September 27, 2024: Last day to turn in AP Fee Reduction Forms (Room 557) - November 6, 2024: Last day to pay for your AP Exam (yearlong and Fall semester classes). After this, a $40 late fee to pay and a $40 cancellation fee. - March 5, 2025: Last day to pay for Spring-only semester classes; last day to cancel an exam with a $40 cancellation fee (no refund after this day); last day to order an exam with a $40 late order fee. How is Transcripts related to Transcripts?

**Page title:** Transcripts Current and past students access official transcripts through Docufide by Parchment, which enables users to instantly send transcripts to institutions of choice. To register accurately through Parchment, the following is required: use ONLY ONE email address per student; register using your LEGAL NAME; current residence address; date of birth; year of graduation. Contact **Mrs. Han** if your request is not processed after 2 business days. Using Docufide enables users to request transcripts for personal use and to send to universities, scholarship foundations, and/or the NCAA Eligibility Center. **Special Note for Current Seniors:** Final transcripts are requested by the student using Parchment. Seniors must follow the steps to request their FINAL transcript to be sent to the university they will attend. This is not done directly by DBHS or the GLC — it will only be sent if the student has requested the final transcript through Parchment. - Register & order transcripts: www.parchment.com - Direct link: http://www.parchment.com/c/college/high-school-2725-Diamond-Bar-High-School.html ---

How is February related to Brahma Tech Academy (PLTW / Engineering)?

**Page title:** Brahma Tech Academy – Welcome Apply at brahma-tech.com/apply (deadline referenced: Thursday, February 26, 2026). More info: brahma-tech.com. **Program Overview:** Brahma Tech Academy students cultivate their STEM identity, develop expertise, and are empowered to showcase their STEM leadership. Students distinguish themselves through concentrated engagement in STEM courses and extracurriculars, culminating in a 150-hour internship in their chosen field. Brahma Tech graduates are qualified for entry into 4-year universities and possess technical and professional skills in demand by industry leaders. The curriculum spans four years. 9th grade consists of an entry-level course in (1) Innovations in Technology, (2) Intro to Engineering Design (PLTW), or (3) AP Computer Science Principles. In grades 10–12, students take courses in 7 different strands. The program concludes with a 150-hour internship. Students who complete the program receive a Brahma Tech STEM certificate and wear the academy stole at graduation. Coordinators: **Barbara Pentangelo** and **Jeremiah Wai**. (The "7 Strands" course list is hosted in an external Canva document.)
How is National related to Suicide Prevention?

**Page title:** Suicide Prevention - **By phone:** National Suicide Prevention Hotline: (800) 273-8255 or 988 — https://suicidepreventionlifeline.org/ - **By text:** Text HOME to 741741 — https://www.crisistextline.org/ - **Dial or text 988.** 988 reaches the Lifeline — a network of more than 200 state and local call centers funded by the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services through SAMHSA and administered by Vibrant Emotional Health. Service is provided in English and Spanish.
What information is available regarding Wellness Center?

**Page title:** Wellness Center **DBHS Wellness Center — "Empowering Student Wellness from Within"** ### Mission Statement The Diamond Bar High School Wellness Staff is committed to creating a supportive environment for students by providing wellness education and resources. Peer counselors are equipped to assist students by guiding them to develop healthy strategies for personal well-being, which promotes individual academic success and improves the overall school climate. ### Vision Statement The Wellness Staff continually strives to be responsive to the overall needs of all students. They believe the physical, emotional, social, intellectual, cultural, and spiritual dimensions of students are the foundation of a well-balanced approach to personal success. The Wellness Center is an inclusive, nonjudgmental space available for all students seeking additional support and/or access to wellness services, education, and resources. **Location:** Room #254. Opened January 2016. Drop by during periods 1–4. Related items: Welcome to Wellness; Wellness Center Calendar; "Students @ Thrive: A Wellness Forum"; therapy dog "Daisy" visits Mondays and Thursdays.

What is Vision, Mission & SLOs?

**Page title:** Vision, Mission & SLOs ### Vision Statement Diamond Bar High School will be a premier school committed to the intellectual, physical, and social development of every student. Diamond Bar will be a community where every student's cultural heritage enriches the learning experience for all students. ### Mission Diamond Bar will value the five "A's" of Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics so that each student will experience a personalized educational program selected from a rigorous, rich and varied curriculum. Students will be challenged to discover their unique gifts and talents; staff members will support every student in realizing his or her potential. Working in partnership with parents and the community, the Diamond Bar High School staff will provide learning opportunities that reach beyond the boundaries of the classroom and ensure every student a chance for success in college and career, and as citizens and leaders of the 21st century. Related links: WASC; SARC; Vision, Mission & SLOs; SLO poster (PDF).

What services are provided by Activities & United Student Body (USB)?

**Page title:** About USB / Activities (USB) **Motto:** "Raising the Bar" United Student Body (USB) is the student government at Diamond Bar High School. USB seeks to improve campus life and help students. USB is responsible for many on-campus activities and programs including lunchtime activities, rallies, dances, Student Store, and clubs approvals/budgets. The term USB was chosen over ASB during the 1982 student body convention. USB has 6 executive board members and 34 commissioners. The term of office is from January to December. Members are enrolled in 4th period Leadership class. **How to Join:** Executive board (e-board) candidates are first approved by the director, **Mrs. (Janna) Van Horn**, and then elected. How is candidates related to IB Diploma Requirements?

**Page title:** IB Diploma Requirements To earn an International Baccalaureate diploma, students must: **Complete courses in six academic subjects, one from each of the IB six subject groups:** - IB diploma candidates must select one subject from each of the six subject groups; - a second subject from groups 3 or 4 may be substituted for a subject in group 6; - three IB subjects must be taken at the higher level (HL), and three at the standard level (SL). A student is also permitted to take 4 HL and 2 SL courses. - A student is only permitted to complete a maximum of two SL courses during junior year. **Complete the core elements:** - the extended essay, a personal research paper; - the Theory of Knowledge, a senior seminar course designed to connect all of the disciplines; - CAS, a student action requirement requiring extra-curricular work in creativity, activity, and service. IB students must meet 7 learning outcomes with their CAS projects. All of the above must be done during students' junior and senior years. **Academic standards:** - Freshmen and sophomores: All students in DBHS IB prep must maintain a 3.0 or higher cumulative GPA in the Groups 1–5 academic courses — reviewed

Guest, MSN, RN, NCSN — Phone: 951-453-3316, Fax: 909-895-8301 - **Health Clerk:** Evelyn Maldonado — Phone: (909) 594-1405 ext. 33110 ### Immunization Requirements Before any child under age 18 is admitted to any pre-kindergarten facility or (TK/K-12) school in California, public or private, California law (17 CCR section 6065) requires a parent or guardian to present an immunization record. The record must: identify the student by name & date of birth; show the month, date, and year each required vaccine was received; have the type of vaccine; and include the name of the physician or agency who gave the vaccine. (Reference: California Immunization Handbook 2019, pg. 5.) Resources: www.ShotsForSchool.org ### Medication During School Hours California Education Code Section 49423 allows the school nurse or other designated school personnel to assist students who must take medication during the school day. Provide information about Work Permits.

**Page title:** Work Permits Students under the age of eighteen (18) must have a work permit on file with the school and their employer. IT'S THE LAW! ### To apply for a work permit: 1. Once you have been hired by an employer: - Complete the Minor's Information section - Complete the School Information Section - Have the application signed by the minor's parent/legal guardian - Have the employer complete and sign the Employer Information section. 2. To turn in a completed work permit, go see **Ms. Ayala** in the Career Center, located inside the Express Library (Rm. 265). Processing a work permit can take 1–2 business days. ### Work Permit Renewals If you are under the age of 18, work permits must be renewed each year. Follow the instructions above to renew. ### Young Workers Health and Safety Information Visit http://www.youngworkers.org/ for the basics about young worker health and safety, with a downloadable fact sheet "Are You A Working Teen?" in English and Spanish. Contact **Mrs. Han** if your request is not processed after 2 business days. Using Docufide enables users to request transcripts for personal use and to send to universities, scholarship foundations, and/or the NCAA Eligibility Center. **Special Note for Current Seniors:** Final transcripts are requested by the student using Parchment. Seniors must follow the steps to request their FINAL transcript to be sent to the university they will attend. This is not done directly by DBHS or the GLC — it will only be sent if the student has requested the final transcript through Parchment. - Register & order transcripts: www.parchment.com - Direct link: http://www.parchment.com/c/college/high-school-2725-Diamond-Bar-High-School.html ---

What should students know about Student Fees?

**Page title:** Student Fees (Fee Info) The California Constitution mandates that public education be provided to students free of charge, unless a charge is specifically authorized by law for a particular program or activity. This constitutional right of free access encompasses all educational activities, whether curricular or extracurricular, and regardless of whether credit is awarded for the educational activity. The right of free access also prohibits mandated purchases of materials, supplies, equipment or uniforms associated with the activity, as well as the payment of security deposits for access, participation, materials or equipment. A process that allows for a waiver process for an otherwise mandatory fee, charge or deposit does not render it constitutionally permissible. Further information (district): http://www.wvusd.k12.ca.us/apps/pages/index.jsp?uREC_ID=298183&type=d

What are the requirements related to Wellness Center?

**Page title:** Wellness Center **DBHS Wellness Center — "Empowering Student Wellness from Within"** ### Mission Statement The Diamond Bar High School Wellness Staff is committed to creating a supportive environment for students by providing wellness education and resources. Peer counselors are equipped to assist students by guiding them to develop healthy strategies for personal well-being, which promotes individual academic success and improves the overall school climate. ### Vision Statement The Wellness Staff continually strives to be responsive to the overall needs of all students. They believe the physical, emotional, social, intellectual, cultural, and spiritual dimensions of students are the foundation of a well-balanced approach to personal success. The Wellness Center is an inclusive, nonjudgmental space available for all students seeking additional support and/or access to wellness services, education, and resources. **Location:** Room #254. Opened January 2016. Drop by during periods 1–4. Related items: Welcome to Wellness; Wellness Center Calendar; "Students @ Thrive: A Wellness Forum"; therapy dog "Daisy" visits Mondays and Thursdays.
What should a new student know about Mathematics Department?

**Page title:** DBHS Mathematics! Welcome to Brahma Mathematics. - 2011, 2013, 2014, 2016, 2022 Regional Champions for the California Mathematics League - 2005 and 2007 AP Calculus World Champions - Winner of the prestigious 2007-2008 Siemens Award for Advanced Placement Any student who wants a recommendation letter from a math teacher for a summer off-campus opportunity or scholarship must submit a request to that teacher no later than THREE WEEKS before the application due date. Each math teacher has their own requirements; check at least FIVE WEEKS prior to the due date. Math section sub-pages: DBHS Mathematics!; Math Help!; Math Pathways; Math Team; Math Team Files; California Mathematics League; Math Madness!; American Mathematics Competition; Are you ready for Algebra II / Trigonometry Honors?

Summarize Grade Level Coordinators.

**Page title:** Grade Level Coordinators (Guidance home) A GLC is a Grade Level Coordinator. Each grade level has two GLCs that assist each student. Grade Level Coordinators act as parents on campus. They handle conflicts that may occur with any of the students, they can assist with what classes to take and what college admission policies are, and they can give guidance to any student looking for someone to talk to. Grade Level Coordinators are out on campus during Brunch and Lunch and invite students to contact them during this time for any assistance needed. ### Grade Level Coordinator Assignments | Group | Coordinators | | --- | --- | | Pathways Academy | Ms. Brummett | | Class of 2026 | Mr. Desmond and Mrs. Tumbeiro | | Class of 2027 | Mr. Gonzales and Mrs. Burns | | Class of 2028 | Ms. Lee and Mrs. Zimmerman | | Class of 2029 | Mr. Galeener and Mrs. Duenas | | Class of 2030 | Mr. Desmond and Ms. Brummett | **School Code:** To sign up for the SAT, use the College Board website. Diamond Bar High School's CEEB Code is **050748** — required to register for tests and complete college applications. **California Proficiency Program (CPP) – HiSET Proficiency Exam:** https://www.cde.ca

Explain Attendance Office in detail.

**Page title:** Attendance Office **Absence line:** (909) 594-1860. **Attendance Clerks:** - **Amber Luna** — Attendance Clerk, students A–Lin — (909) 594-1405 ext. 33124 - **Jennifer Chung** — Attendance Clerk, students Lio–Z — (909) 594-1405 ext. 33123 ### Attendance FAQ **How to report an absence/tardy:** Absences must be cleared within 3 days; tardies/late arrivals within 1 day. Methods: - **Email** — include student's full name, student ID number, grade level, date(s) of absence, and reason. - **Note** — sent with the student; include full name, ID number, grade, date(s), reason, and parent signature. - **Call the attendance office** at the clerk extensions above. **Voicemail:** If you reach voicemail, leave: student's full name, student ID number, date of absence/tardy/early leave, and specific reason. (To excuse a student early, refer to early-leave procedures.) Failure to provide this information may result in incorrect recording. **Incorrectly marked absent:** Students should monitor attendance via Aeries regularly or obtain a printout from the Attendance Office. Why is Wellness Center important?

**Page title:** Wellness Center **DBHS Wellness Center — "Empowering Student Wellness from Within"** ### Mission Statement The Diamond Bar High School Wellness Staff is committed to creating a supportive environment for students by providing wellness education and resources. Peer counselors are equipped to assist students by guiding them to develop healthy strategies for personal well-being, which promotes individual academic success and improves the overall school climate. ### Vision Statement The Wellness Staff continually strives to be responsive to the overall needs of all students. They believe the physical, emotional, social, intellectual, cultural, and spiritual dimensions of students are the foundation of a well-balanced approach to personal success. The Wellness Center is an inclusive, nonjudgmental space available for all students seeking additional support and/or access to wellness services, education, and resources. **Location:** Room #254. Opened January 2016. Drop by during periods 1–4. Related items: Welcome to Wellness; Wellness Center Calendar; "Students @ Thrive: A Wellness Forum"; therapy dog "Daisy" visits Mondays and Thursdays.

How is develop related to International Baccalaureate (IB)?

**Page title:** IB Program The International Baccalaureate (IB) aims to develop inquiring, knowledgeable and caring young people who help to create a better and more peaceful world through intercultural understanding and respect. The IB has four programs for students aged 3-19. These programs encourage students across the world to become active, compassionate and lifelong learners. They empower students to take ownership in their own learning and help them develop the knowledge, skills, and sense of purpose they need to thrive throughout their lives. Diamond Bar High School offers the IB Diploma Program (DP). The IBDP is a challenging two-year curriculum, primarily aimed at students aged 16 to 19. It leads to a qualification widely recognized by the world's leading universities. The program prepares students for university and encourages them to: ask challenging questions; learn how to learn; develop a strong sense of their own identity and culture; and develop the ability to communicate with and understand people from other countries and cultures. ### Donations The DBHS IB program accepts donations to support IB student activities, social events, and classroom supplies. **Page title:** About USB / Activities (USB) **Motto:** "Raising the Bar" United Student Body (USB) is the student government at Diamond Bar High School. USB seeks to improve campus life and help students. USB is responsible for many on-campus activities and programs including lunchtime activities, rallies, dances, Student Store, and clubs approvals/budgets. The term USB was chosen over ASB during the 1982 student body convention. USB has 6 executive board members and 34 commissioners. The term of office is from January to December. Members are enrolled in 4th period Leadership class. **How to Join:** Executive board (e-board) candidates are first approved by the director, **Mrs. (Janna) Van Horn**, and then elected. Why is Health Office important?

**Page title:** Health Office - **School Nurse:** Theresa A. Guest, MSN, RN, NCSN — Phone: 951-453-3316, Fax: 909-895-8301 - **Health Clerk:** Evelyn Maldonado — Phone: (909) 594-1405 ext. 33110 ### Immunization Requirements Before any child under age 18 is admitted to any pre-kindergarten facility or (TK/K-12) school in California, public or private, California law (17 CCR section 6065) requires a parent or guardian to present an immunization record. The record must: identify the student by name & date of birth; show the month, date, and year each required vaccine was received; have the type of vaccine; and include the name of the physician or agency who gave the vaccine. (Reference: California Immunization Handbook 2019, pg. 5.) Resources: www.ShotsForSchool.org ### Medication During School Hours California Education Code Section 49423 allows the school nurse or other designated school personnel to assist students who must take medication during the school day. If a physician/dentist visit may result in prescribed medication or a dosage change, stop by the school office or download the Medication Required During School Consent. ### Health Office Forms Allergy Questionnaire; Asthm

What are the most important points about IB Diploma Requirements?

**Page title:** IB Diploma Requirements To earn an International Baccalaureate diploma, students must: **Complete courses in six academic subjects, one from each of the IB six subject groups:** - IB diploma candidates must select one subject from each of the six subject groups; - a second subject from groups 3 or 4 may be substituted for a subject in group 6; - three IB subjects must be taken at the higher level (HL), and three at the standard level (SL). A student is also permitted to take 4 HL and 2 SL courses. - A student is only permitted to complete a maximum of two SL courses during junior year. **Complete the core elements:** - the extended essay, a personal research paper; - the Theory of Knowledge, a senior seminar course designed to connect all of the disciplines; - CAS, a student action requirement requiring extra-curricular work in creativity, activity, and service. IB students must meet 7 learning outcomes with their CAS projects. All of the above must be done during students' junior and senior years. **Academic standards:** - Freshmen and sophomores: All students in DBHS IB prep must maintain a 3.0 or higher cumulative GPA in the Groups 1–5 academic courses — reviewed

**Page title:** Transcripts Current and past students access official transcripts through Docufide by Parchment, which enables users to instantly send transcripts to institutions of choice. To register accurately through Parchment, the following is required: use ONLY ONE email address per student; register using your LEGAL NAME; current residence address; date of birth; year of graduation. Contact **Mrs. Han** if your request is not processed after 2 business days. Using Docufide enables users to request transcripts for personal use and to send to universities, scholarship foundations, and/or the NCAA Eligibility Center. **Special Note for Current Seniors:** Final transcripts are requested by the student using Parchment. Seniors must follow the steps to request their FINAL transcript to be sent to the university they will attend. This is not done directly by DBHS or the GLC — it will only be sent if the student has requested the final transcript through Parchment. - Register & order transcripts: www.parchment.com - Direct link: http://www.parchment.com/c/college/high-school-2725-Diamond-Bar-High-School.html ---

How is California related to Student Fees?

**Page title:** Student Fees (Fee Info) The California Constitution mandates that public education be provided to students free of charge, unless a charge is specifically authorized by law for a particular program or activity. This constitutional right of free access encompasses all educational activities, whether curricular or extracurricular, and regardless of whether credit is awarded for the educational activity. The right of free access also prohibits mandated purchases of materials, supplies, equipment or uniforms associated with the activity, as well as the payment of security deposits for access, participation, materials or equipment. A process that allows for a waiver process for an otherwise mandatory fee, charge or deposit does not render it constitutionally permissible. Further information (district): http://www.wvusd.k12.ca.us/apps/pages/index.jsp?uREC_ID=298183&type=d

How is committed related to Wellness Center?

**Page title:** Wellness Center **DBHS Wellness Center — "Empowering Student Wellness from Within"** ### Mission Statement The Diamond Bar High School Wellness Staff is committed to creating a supportive environment for students by providing wellness education and resources. Peer counselors are equipped to assist students by guiding them to develop healthy strategies for personal well-being, which promotes individual academic success and improves the overall school climate. ### Vision Statement The Wellness Staff continually strives to be responsive to the overall needs of all students. They believe the physical, emotional, social, intellectual, cultural, and spiritual dimensions of students are the foundation of a well-balanced approach to personal success. The Wellness Center is an inclusive, nonjudgmental space available for all students seeking additional support and/or access to wellness services, education, and resources. **Location:** Room #254. Opened January 2016. Drop by during periods 1–4. Related items: Welcome to Wellness; Wellness Center Calendar; "Students @ Thrive: A Wellness Forum"; therapy dog "Daisy" visits Mondays and Thursdays.
What is Student Fees?

**Page title:** Student Fees (Fee Info) The California Constitution mandates that public education be provided to students free of charge, unless a charge is specifically authorized by law for a particular program or activity. This constitutional right of free access encompasses all educational activities, whether curricular or extracurricular, and regardless of whether credit is awarded for the educational activity. The right of free access also prohibits mandated purchases of materials, supplies, equipment or uniforms associated with the activity, as well as the payment of security deposits for access, participation, materials or equipment. A process that allows for a waiver process for an otherwise mandatory fee, charge or deposit does not render it constitutionally permissible. Further information (district): http://www.wvusd.k12.ca.us/apps/pages/index.jsp?uREC_ID=298183&type=d

How can a student use IB Diploma Requirements?

**Page title:** IB Diploma Requirements To earn an International Baccalaureate diploma, students must: **Complete courses in six academic subjects, one from each of the IB six subject groups:** - IB diploma candidates must select one subject from each of the six subject groups; - a second subject from groups 3 or 4 may be substituted for a subject in group 6; - three IB subjects must be taken at the higher level (HL), and three at the standard level (SL). A student is also permitted to take 4 HL and 2 SL courses. - A student is only permitted to complete a maximum of two SL courses during junior year. **Complete the core elements:** - the extended essay, a personal research paper; - the Theory of Knowledge, a senior seminar course designed to connect all of the disciplines; - CAS, a student action requirement requiring extra-curricular work in creativity, activity, and service. IB students must meet 7 learning outcomes with their CAS projects. All of the above must be done during students' junior and senior years. **Academic standards:** - Freshmen and sophomores: All students in DBHS IB prep must maintain a 3.0 or higher cumulative GPA in the Groups 1–5 academic courses — reviewed

How is through related to Awards & Recognition?

**Page title:** Awards — U.S. News & World Report's Best High Schools U.S. News & World Reports - Best High Schools: recognized 2008–2024 (every year listed 2008 through 2024). US News & World Report published their high school rankings for 2024-25 on Tuesday, April 23. Once again, DBHS was ranked among the best schools in the state and the nation. The U.S. News Best High Schools rankings include data on nearly 25,000 public high schools in 50 states and the District of Columbia. Nearly 18,000 schools were ranked on six factors based on their performance on state assessments and how well they prepare students for college. **Rankings (2024-25):** - #408 in National Rankings - #56 in California High Schools - #21 in Los Angeles, CA Metro Area High Schools - #149 in STEM High Schools DBHS's rankings are a result of a partnership among students, staff, parents, community members, and the district office in supporting the balance provided by the four A's: Academics, Activities, the Arts, and Athletics. At the center of these foundations is the belief in providing equitable access to opportunities for success to ALL students. **The six ranking factors:** | Factor | Weight | Description |

**Page title:** About USB / Activities (USB) **Motto:** "Raising the Bar" United Student Body (USB) is the student government at Diamond Bar High School. USB seeks to improve campus life and help students. USB is responsible for many on-campus activities and programs including lunchtime activities, rallies, dances, Student Store, and clubs approvals/budgets. The term USB was chosen over ASB during the 1982 student body convention. USB has 6 executive board members and 34 commissioners. The term of office is from January to December. Members are enrolled in 4th period Leadership class. **How to Join:** Executive board (e-board) candidates are first approved by the director, **Mrs. (Janna) Van Horn**, and then elected. How is students related to Brahma Tech Academy (PLTW / Engineering)?

**Page title:** Brahma Tech Academy – Welcome Apply at brahma-tech.com/apply (deadline referenced: Thursday, February 26, 2026). More info: brahma-tech.com. **Program Overview:** Brahma Tech Academy students cultivate their STEM identity, develop expertise, and are empowered to showcase their STEM leadership. Students distinguish themselves through concentrated engagement in STEM courses and extracurriculars, culminating in a 150-hour internship in their chosen field. Brahma Tech graduates are qualified for entry into 4-year universities and possess technical and professional skills in demand by industry leaders. The curriculum spans four years. 9th grade consists of an entry-level course in (1) Innovations in Technology, (2) Intro to Engineering Design (PLTW), or (3) AP Computer Science Principles. In grades 10–12, students take courses in 7 different strands. The program concludes with a 150-hour internship. Students who complete the program receive a Brahma Tech STEM certificate and wear the academy stole at graduation. Coordinators: **Barbara Pentangelo** and **Jeremiah Wai**. (The "7 Strands" course list is hosted in an external Canva document.)

Provide a complete summary of Student Fees.

**Page title:** Student Fees (Fee Info) The California Constitution mandates that public education be provided to students free of charge, unless a charge is specifically authorized by law for a particular program or activity. This constitutional right of free access encompasses all educational activities, whether curricular or extracurricular, and regardless of whether credit is awarded for the educational activity. The right of free access also prohibits mandated purchases of materials, supplies, equipment or uniforms associated with the activity, as well as the payment of security deposits for access, participation, materials or equipment. A process that allows for a waiver process for an otherwise mandatory fee, charge or deposit does not render it constitutionally permissible. Further information (district): http://www.wvusd.k12.ca.us/apps/pages/index.jsp?uREC_ID=298183&type=d

What should a new student know about Work Permits?

**Page title:** Work Permits Students under the age of eighteen (18) must have a work permit on file with the school and their employer. IT'S THE LAW! ### To apply for a work permit: 1. Once you have been hired by an employer: - Complete the Minor's Information section - Complete the School Information Section - Have the application signed by the minor's parent/legal guardian - Have the employer complete and sign the Employer Information section. 2. To turn in a completed work permit, go see **Ms. Ayala** in the Career Center, located inside the Express Library (Rm. 265). Processing a work permit can take 1–2 business days. ### Work Permit Renewals If you are under the age of 18, work permits must be renewed each year. Follow the instructions above to renew. ### Young Workers Health and Safety Information Visit http://www.youngworkers.org/ for the basics about young worker health and safety, with a downloadable fact sheet "Are You A Working Teen?" in English and Spanish. Linked documents: DBHS Work Permit (PDF); CA Child Labor Laws (https://www.dir.ca.gov/dlse/childlaborlawpamphlet.pdf).

What does the DBHS knowledge base say about History & Community?

**Page title:** School Info Diamond Bar High School (DBHS) opened in 1982 and is located in the city of Diamond Bar, California. The southern portion of the city is served by the Walnut Valley Unified School District, while the northern portion is served by the Pomona Unified School District. DBHS is one of two comprehensive high schools in the Walnut Valley Unified School District. The district also offers a continuation high school, three middle schools and nine elementary schools. Diamond Bar is a multi-ethnic, upper middle-class bedroom community stretching along the 57 and 60 freeways on the eastern edge of Los Angeles County. In 2021, the population of Diamond Bar was 53,857, the median income was $100,730, and in 2017, the median age was 38 years. The city is served by two different school districts, Pomona Unified on the north side and Walnut Valley Unified on the southern side. Diamond Bar is also an affluent and aging community. Since 2014, the segment of the population who ethnically identifies as white decreased from 30% to 21.7% and the Asian/Pacific Islander population has remained stable at 53.1%. What information is available regarding Awards & Recognition?

**Page title:** Awards — U.S. News & World Report's Best High Schools U.S. News & World Reports - Best High Schools: recognized 2008–2024 (every year listed 2008 through 2024). US News & World Report published their high school rankings for 2024-25 on Tuesday, April 23. Once again, DBHS was ranked among the best schools in the state and the nation. The U.S. News Best High Schools rankings include data on nearly 25,000 public high schools in 50 states and the District of Columbia. Nearly 18,000 schools were ranked on six factors based on their performance on state assessments and how well they prepare students for college. **Rankings (2024-25):** - #408 in National Rankings - #56 in California High Schools - #21 in Los Angeles, CA Metro Area High Schools - #149 in STEM High Schools DBHS's rankings are a result of a partnership among students, staff, parents, community members, and the district office in supporting the balance provided by the four A's: Academics, Activities, the Arts, and Athletics. At the center of these foundations is the belief in providing equitable access to opportunities for success to ALL students. **The six ranking factors:** | Factor | Weight | Description |

What procedures are associated with Transcripts?

**Page title:** Transcripts Current and past students access official transcripts through Docufide by Parchment, which enables users to instantly send transcripts to institutions of choice. To register accurately through Parchment, the following is required: use ONLY ONE email address per student; register using your LEGAL NAME; current residence address; date of birth; year of graduation. Contact **Mrs. Han** if your request is not processed after 2 business days. Using Docufide enables users to request transcripts for personal use and to send to universities, scholarship foundations, and/or the NCAA Eligibility Center. **Special Note for Current Seniors:** Final transcripts are requested by the student using Parchment. Seniors must follow the steps to request their FINAL transcript to be sent to the university they will attend. This is not done directly by DBHS or the GLC — it will only be sent if the student has requested the final transcript through Parchment. - Register & order transcripts: www.parchment.com - Direct link: http://www.parchment.com/c/college/high-school-2725-Diamond-Bar-High-School.html ---

Provide a complete summary of Activities & United Student Body (USB).

**Page title:** About USB / Activities (USB) **Motto:** "Raising the Bar" United Student Body (USB) is the student government at Diamond Bar High School. USB seeks to improve campus life and help students. USB is responsible for many on-campus activities and programs including lunchtime activities, rallies, dances, Student Store, and clubs approvals/budgets. The term USB was chosen over ASB during the 1982 student body convention. USB has 6 executive board members and 34 commissioners. The term of office is from January to December. Members are enrolled in 4th period Leadership class. **How to Join:** Executive board (e-board) candidates are first approved by the director, **Mrs. (Janna) Van Horn**, and then elected. Commissioners are selected by the executive board through a paper application and two interviews. ### USB 2025 Members **Activities Director:** Janna Van Horn **Executive Board** | Position | Member | | --- | --- | | USB President | Miles Lai | | Vice President | Daniel Lee | | Speaker of the House | Matthew Giang | | Finance Director | Michelle Park | | IOC | Noah Gonzales | | Secretary | Alena Mangsat | **Class Presidents (as printed)** | Position | Member | | --- |
How can a student use Grade Level Coordinators?

**Page title:** Grade Level Coordinators (Guidance home) A GLC is a Grade Level Coordinator. Each grade level has two GLCs that assist each student. Grade Level Coordinators act as parents on campus. They handle conflicts that may occur with any of the students, they can assist with what classes to take and what college admission policies are, and they can give guidance to any student looking for someone to talk to. Grade Level Coordinators are out on campus during Brunch and Lunch and invite students to contact them during this time for any assistance needed. ### Grade Level Coordinator Assignments | Group | Coordinators | | --- | --- | | Pathways Academy | Ms. Brummett | | Class of 2026 | Mr. Desmond and Mrs. Tumbeiro | | Class of 2027 | Mr. Gonzales and Mrs. Burns | | Class of 2028 | Ms. Lee and Mrs. Zimmerman | | Class of 2029 | Mr. Galeener and Mrs. Duenas | | Class of 2030 | Mr. Desmond and Ms. Brummett | **School Code:** To sign up for the SAT, use the College Board website. Diamond Bar High School's CEEB Code is **050748** — required to register for tests and complete college applications. **California Proficiency Program (CPP) – HiSET Proficiency Exam:** https://www.cde.ca

This page focuses on AP exam registration and payment logistics (it does not enumerate the individual AP courses offered). ### AP Updates - Log in to your AP Classroom if you have not already (your teacher provides a join code). If your class period changed and you are in the wrong AP classroom, email Jennifer Burns. - Use your personal email address and legal name when registering. If you used your school email or a nickname, contact College Board to change it. College Board phone: (888) 225-5427, M–F 6am–3pm. - Once registered in AP Classroom, pay for your exam through the DBHS Webstore. (Do not pay for an exam for a class you are not enrolled in.) ### Important AP Dates (as published) - September 27, 2024: Last day to turn in AP Fee Reduction Forms (Room 557) - November 6, 2024: Last day to pay for your AP Exam (yearlong and Fall semester classes). After this, a $40 late fee to pay and a $40 cancellation fee. - March 5, 2025: Last day to pay for Spring-only semester classes; last day to cancel an exam with a $40 cancellation fee (no refund after this day); last day to order an exam with a $40 late order fee. What are the key details about History & Community?

**Page title:** School Info Diamond Bar High School (DBHS) opened in 1982 and is located in the city of Diamond Bar, California. The southern portion of the city is served by the Walnut Valley Unified School District, while the northern portion is served by the Pomona Unified School District. DBHS is one of two comprehensive high schools in the Walnut Valley Unified School District. The district also offers a continuation high school, three middle schools and nine elementary schools. Diamond Bar is a multi-ethnic, upper middle-class bedroom community stretching along the 57 and 60 freeways on the eastern edge of Los Angeles County. In 2021, the population of Diamond Bar was 53,857, the median income was $100,730, and in 2017, the median age was 38 years. The city is served by two different school districts, Pomona Unified on the north side and Walnut Valley Unified on the southern side. Diamond Bar is also an affluent and aging community. Since 2014, the segment of the population who ethnically identifies as white decreased from 30% to 21.7% and the Asian/Pacific Islander population has remained stable at 53.1%. How is subjects related to IB Diploma Requirements?

**Page title:** IB Diploma Requirements To earn an International Baccalaureate diploma, students must: **Complete courses in six academic subjects, one from each of the IB six subject groups:** - IB diploma candidates must select one subject from each of the six subject groups; - a second subject from groups 3 or 4 may be substituted for a subject in group 6; - three IB subjects must be taken at the higher level (HL), and three at the standard level (SL). A student is also permitted to take 4 HL and 2 SL courses. - A student is only permitted to complete a maximum of two SL courses during junior year. **Complete the core elements:** - the extended essay, a personal research paper; - the Theory of Knowledge, a senior seminar course designed to connect all of the disciplines; - CAS, a student action requirement requiring extra-curricular work in creativity, activity, and service. IB students must meet 7 learning outcomes with their CAS projects. All of the above must be done during students' junior and senior years. **Academic standards:** - Freshmen and sophomores: All students in DBHS IB prep must maintain a 3.0 or higher cumulative GPA in the Groups 1–5 academic courses — reviewed

How is California related to History & Community?

**Page title:** School Info Diamond Bar High School (DBHS) opened in 1982 and is located in the city of Diamond Bar, California. The southern portion of the city is served by the Walnut Valley Unified School District, while the northern portion is served by the Pomona Unified School District. DBHS is one of two comprehensive high schools in the Walnut Valley Unified School District. The district also offers a continuation high school, three middle schools and nine elementary schools. Diamond Bar is a multi-ethnic, upper middle-class bedroom community stretching along the 57 and 60 freeways on the eastern edge of Los Angeles County. In 2021, the population of Diamond Bar was 53,857, the median income was $100,730, and in 2017, the median age was 38 years. The city is served by two different school districts, Pomona Unified on the north side and Walnut Valley Unified on the southern side. Diamond Bar is also an affluent and aging community. Since 2014, the segment of the population who ethnically identifies as white decreased from 30% to 21.7% and the Asian/Pacific Islander population has remained stable at 53.1%. Summarize Student Fees.

**Page title:** Student Fees (Fee Info) The California Constitution mandates that public education be provided to students free of charge, unless a charge is specifically authorized by law for a particular program or activity. This constitutional right of free access encompasses all educational activities, whether curricular or extracurricular, and regardless of whether credit is awarded for the educational activity. The right of free access also prohibits mandated purchases of materials, supplies, equipment or uniforms associated with the activity, as well as the payment of security deposits for access, participation, materials or equipment. A process that allows for a waiver process for an otherwise mandatory fee, charge or deposit does not render it constitutionally permissible. Further information (district): http://www.wvusd.k12.ca.us/apps/pages/index.jsp?uREC_ID=298183&type=d

How does Brahma Tech Academy (PLTW / Engineering) work?

**Page title:** Brahma Tech Academy – Welcome Apply at brahma-tech.com/apply (deadline referenced: Thursday, February 26, 2026). More info: brahma-tech.com. **Program Overview:** Brahma Tech Academy students cultivate their STEM identity, develop expertise, and are empowered to showcase their STEM leadership. Students distinguish themselves through concentrated engagement in STEM courses and extracurriculars, culminating in a 150-hour internship in their chosen field. Brahma Tech graduates are qualified for entry into 4-year universities and possess technical and professional skills in demand by industry leaders. The curriculum spans four years. 9th grade consists of an entry-level course in (1) Innovations in Technology, (2) Intro to Engineering Design (PLTW), or (3) AP Computer Science Principles. In grades 10–12, students take courses in 7 different strands. The program concludes with a 150-hour internship. Students who complete the program receive a Brahma Tech STEM certificate and wear the academy stole at graduation. Coordinators: **Barbara Pentangelo** and **Jeremiah Wai**. (The "7 Strands" course list is hosted in an external Canva document.)

Tell me about International Baccalaureate (IB).

**Page title:** IB Program The International Baccalaureate (IB) aims to develop inquiring, knowledgeable and caring young people who help to create a better and more peaceful world through intercultural understanding and respect. The IB has four programs for students aged 3-19. These programs encourage students across the world to become active, compassionate and lifelong learners. They empower students to take ownership in their own learning and help them develop the knowledge, skills, and sense of purpose they need to thrive throughout their lives. Diamond Bar High School offers the IB Diploma Program (DP). The IBDP is a challenging two-year curriculum, primarily aimed at students aged 16 to 19. It leads to a qualification widely recognized by the world's leading universities. The program prepares students for university and encourages them to: ask challenging questions; learn how to learn; develop a strong sense of their own identity and culture; and develop the ability to communicate with and understand people from other countries and cultures. ### Donations The DBHS IB program accepts donations to support IB student activities, social events, and classroom supplies. How is diploma related to IB Diploma Requirements?

**Page title:** IB Diploma Requirements To earn an International Baccalaureate diploma, students must: **Complete courses in six academic subjects, one from each of the IB six subject groups:** - IB diploma candidates must select one subject from each of the six subject groups; - a second subject from groups 3 or 4 may be substituted for a subject in group 6; - three IB subjects must be taken at the higher level (HL), and three at the standard level (SL). A student is also permitted to take 4 HL and 2 SL courses. - A student is only permitted to complete a maximum of two SL courses during junior year. **Complete the core elements:** - the extended essay, a personal research paper; - the Theory of Knowledge, a senior seminar course designed to connect all of the disciplines; - CAS, a student action requirement requiring extra-curricular work in creativity, activity, and service. IB students must meet 7 learning outcomes with their CAS projects. All of the above must be done during students' junior and senior years. **Academic standards:** - Freshmen and sophomores: All students in DBHS IB prep must maintain a 3.0 or higher cumulative GPA in the Groups 1–5 academic courses — reviewed

Give me a detailed overview of Vision, Mission & SLOs.

**Page title:** Vision, Mission & SLOs ### Vision Statement Diamond Bar High School will be a premier school committed to the intellectual, physical, and social development of every student. Diamond Bar will be a community where every student's cultural heritage enriches the learning experience for all students. ### Mission Diamond Bar will value the five "A's" of Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics so that each student will experience a personalized educational program selected from a rigorous, rich and varied curriculum. Students will be challenged to discover their unique gifts and talents; staff members will support every student in realizing his or her potential. Working in partnership with parents and the community, the Diamond Bar High School staff will provide learning opportunities that reach beyond the boundaries of the classroom and ensure every student a chance for success in college and career, and as citizens and leaders of the 21st century. Guest, MSN, RN, NCSN — Phone: 951-453-3316, Fax: 909-895-8301 - **Health Clerk:** Evelyn Maldonado — Phone: (909) 594-1405 ext. 33110 ### Immunization Requirements Before any child under age 18 is admitted to any pre-kindergarten facility or (TK/K-12) school in California, public or private, California law (17 CCR section 6065) requires a parent or guardian to present an immunization record. The record must: identify the student by name & date of birth; show the month, date, and year each required vaccine was received; have the type of vaccine; and include the name of the physician or agency who gave the vaccine. (Reference: California Immunization Handbook 2019, pg. 5.) Resources: www.ShotsForSchool.org ### Medication During School Hours California Education Code Section 49423 allows the school nurse or other designated school personnel to assist students who must take medication during the school day. If a physician/dentist visit may result in prescribed medication or a dosage change, stop by the school office or download the Medication Required During School Consent. ### Health Office Forms Allergy Questionnaire; Asthm

Explain Brahma Tech Academy (PLTW / Engineering) in detail.

**Page title:** Brahma Tech Academy – Welcome Apply at brahma-tech.com/apply (deadline referenced: Thursday, February 26, 2026). More info: brahma-tech.com. **Program Overview:** Brahma Tech Academy students cultivate their STEM identity, develop expertise, and are empowered to showcase their STEM leadership. Students distinguish themselves through concentrated engagement in STEM courses and extracurriculars, culminating in a 150-hour internship in their chosen field. Brahma Tech graduates are qualified for entry into 4-year universities and possess technical and professional skills in demand by industry leaders. The curriculum spans four years. 9th grade consists of an entry-level course in (1) Innovations in Technology, (2) Intro to Engineering Design (PLTW), or (3) AP Computer Science Principles. In grades 10–12, students take courses in 7 different strands. The program concludes with a 150-hour internship. Students who complete the program receive a Brahma Tech STEM certificate and wear the academy stole at graduation. Coordinators: **Barbara Pentangelo** and **Jeremiah Wai**. (The "7 Strands" course list is hosted in an external Canva document.)

Explain History & Community in detail.

**Page title:** School Info Diamond Bar High School (DBHS) opened in 1982 and is located in the city of Diamond Bar, California. The southern portion of the city is served by the Walnut Valley Unified School District, while the northern portion is served by the Pomona Unified School District. DBHS is one of two comprehensive high schools in the Walnut Valley Unified School District. The district also offers a continuation high school, three middle schools and nine elementary schools. Diamond Bar is a multi-ethnic, upper middle-class bedroom community stretching along the 57 and 60 freeways on the eastern edge of Los Angeles County. In 2021, the population of Diamond Bar was 53,857, the median income was $100,730, and in 2017, the median age was 38 years. The city is served by two different school districts, Pomona Unified on the north side and Walnut Valley Unified on the southern side. Diamond Bar is also an affluent and aging community. Since 2014, the segment of the population who ethnically identifies as white decreased from 30% to 21.7% and the Asian/Pacific Islander population has remained stable at 53.1%. How is Constitution related to Student Fees?

**Page title:** Student Fees (Fee Info) The California Constitution mandates that public education be provided to students free of charge, unless a charge is specifically authorized by law for a particular program or activity. This constitutional right of free access encompasses all educational activities, whether curricular or extracurricular, and regardless of whether credit is awarded for the educational activity. The right of free access also prohibits mandated purchases of materials, supplies, equipment or uniforms associated with the activity, as well as the payment of security deposits for access, participation, materials or equipment. A process that allows for a waiver process for an otherwise mandatory fee, charge or deposit does not render it constitutionally permissible. Further information (district): http://www.wvusd.k12.ca.us/apps/pages/index.jsp?uREC_ID=298183&type=d

What procedures are associated with Activities & United Student Body (USB)?

**Page title:** About USB / Activities (USB) **Motto:** "Raising the Bar" United Student Body (USB) is the student government at Diamond Bar High School. USB seeks to improve campus life and help students. USB is responsible for many on-campus activities and programs including lunchtime activities, rallies, dances, Student Store, and clubs approvals/budgets. The term USB was chosen over ASB during the 1982 student body convention. USB has 6 executive board members and 34 commissioners. The term of office is from January to December. Members are enrolled in 4th period Leadership class. **How to Join:** Executive board (e-board) candidates are first approved by the director, **Mrs. (Janna) Van Horn**, and then elected. How is title related to Vision, Mission & SLOs?

**Page title:** Vision, Mission & SLOs ### Vision Statement Diamond Bar High School will be a premier school committed to the intellectual, physical, and social development of every student. Diamond Bar will be a community where every student's cultural heritage enriches the learning experience for all students. ### Mission Diamond Bar will value the five "A's" of Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics so that each student will experience a personalized educational program selected from a rigorous, rich and varied curriculum. Students will be challenged to discover their unique gifts and talents; staff members will support every student in realizing his or her potential. Working in partnership with parents and the community, the Diamond Bar High School staff will provide learning opportunities that reach beyond the boundaries of the classroom and ensure every student a chance for success in college and career, and as citizens and leaders of the 21st century. Summarize Wellness Center.

**Page title:** Wellness Center **DBHS Wellness Center — "Empowering Student Wellness from Within"** ### Mission Statement The Diamond Bar High School Wellness Staff is committed to creating a supportive environment for students by providing wellness education and resources. Peer counselors are equipped to assist students by guiding them to develop healthy strategies for personal well-being, which promotes individual academic success and improves the overall school climate. ### Vision Statement The Wellness Staff continually strives to be responsive to the overall needs of all students. They believe the physical, emotional, social, intellectual, cultural, and spiritual dimensions of students are the foundation of a well-balanced approach to personal success. The Wellness Center is an inclusive, nonjudgmental space available for all students seeking additional support and/or access to wellness services, education, and resources. **Location:** Room #254. Opened January 2016. Drop by during periods 1–4. Related items: Welcome to Wellness; Wellness Center Calendar; "Students @ Thrive: A Wellness Forum"; therapy dog "Daisy" visits Mondays and Thursdays.

What should a new student know about International Baccalaureate (IB)?

**Page title:** IB Program The International Baccalaureate (IB) aims to develop inquiring, knowledgeable and caring young people who help to create a better and more peaceful world through intercultural understanding and respect. The IB has four programs for students aged 3-19. These programs encourage students across the world to become active, compassionate and lifelong learners. They empower students to take ownership in their own learning and help them develop the knowledge, skills, and sense of purpose they need to thrive throughout their lives. Diamond Bar High School offers the IB Diploma Program (DP). The IBDP is a challenging two-year curriculum, primarily aimed at students aged 16 to 19. It leads to a qualification widely recognized by the world's leading universities. The program prepares students for university and encourages them to: ask challenging questions; learn how to learn; develop a strong sense of their own identity and culture; and develop the ability to communicate with and understand people from other countries and cultures. ### Donations The DBHS IB program accepts donations to support IB student activities, social events, and classroom supplies. How is https related to Suicide Prevention?

**Page title:** Suicide Prevention - **By phone:** National Suicide Prevention Hotline: (800) 273-8255 or 988 — https://suicidepreventionlifeline.org/ - **By text:** Text HOME to 741741 — https://www.crisistextline.org/ - **Dial or text 988.** 988 reaches the Lifeline — a network of more than 200 state and local call centers funded by the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services through SAMHSA and administered by Vibrant Emotional Health. Service is provided in English and Spanish.
What should students know about Advanced Placement (AP)?

**Page title:** AP Program – Home AP Coordinator: **Jennifer Burns**. This page focuses on AP exam registration and payment logistics (it does not enumerate the individual AP courses offered). ### AP Updates - Log in to your AP Classroom if you have not already (your teacher provides a join code). If your class period changed and you are in the wrong AP classroom, email Jennifer Burns. - Use your personal email address and legal name when registering. If you used your school email or a nickname, contact College Board to change it. College Board phone: (888) 225-5427, M–F 6am–3pm. - Once registered in AP Classroom, pay for your exam through the DBHS Webstore. (Do not pay for an exam for a class you are not enrolled in.) ### Important AP Dates (as published) - September 27, 2024: Last day to turn in AP Fee Reduction Forms (Room 557) - November 6, 2024: Last day to pay for your AP Exam (yearlong and Fall semester classes). After this, a $40 late fee to pay and a $40 cancellation fee. - March 5, 2025: Last day to pay for Spring-only semester classes; last day to cancel an exam with a $40 cancellation fee (no refund after this day); last day to order an exam with a $40 late order fee. How is development related to Vision, Mission & SLOs?

**Page title:** Vision, Mission & SLOs ### Vision Statement Diamond Bar High School will be a premier school committed to the intellectual, physical, and social development of every student. Diamond Bar will be a community where every student's cultural heritage enriches the learning experience for all students. ### Mission Diamond Bar will value the five "A's" of Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics so that each student will experience a personalized educational program selected from a rigorous, rich and varied curriculum. Students will be challenged to discover their unique gifts and talents; staff members will support every student in realizing his or her potential. Working in partnership with parents and the community, the Diamond Bar High School staff will provide learning opportunities that reach beyond the boundaries of the classroom and ensure every student a chance for success in college and career, and as citizens and leaders of the 21st century. Related links: WASC; SARC; Vision, Mission & SLOs; SLO poster (PDF).

What are the most important points about Wellness Center?

**Page title:** Wellness Center **DBHS Wellness Center — "Empowering Student Wellness from Within"** ### Mission Statement The Diamond Bar High School Wellness Staff is committed to creating a supportive environment for students by providing wellness education and resources. Peer counselors are equipped to assist students by guiding them to develop healthy strategies for personal well-being, which promotes individual academic success and improves the overall school climate. ### Vision Statement The Wellness Staff continually strives to be responsive to the overall needs of all students. They believe the physical, emotional, social, intellectual, cultural, and spiritual dimensions of students are the foundation of a well-balanced approach to personal success. The Wellness Center is an inclusive, nonjudgmental space available for all students seeking additional support and/or access to wellness services, education, and resources. **Location:** Room #254. Opened January 2016. Drop by during periods 1–4. Related items: Welcome to Wellness; Wellness Center Calendar; "Students @ Thrive: A Wellness Forum"; therapy dog "Daisy" visits Mondays and Thursdays.
Tell me about Grade Level Coordinators.

**Page title:** Grade Level Coordinators (Guidance home) A GLC is a Grade Level Coordinator. Each grade level has two GLCs that assist each student. Grade Level Coordinators act as parents on campus. They handle conflicts that may occur with any of the students, they can assist with what classes to take and what college admission policies are, and they can give guidance to any student looking for someone to talk to. How is Immunization related to Health Office?

**Page title:** Health Office - **School Nurse:** Theresa A. Guest, MSN, RN, NCSN — Phone: 951-453-3316, Fax: 909-895-8301 - **Health Clerk:** Evelyn Maldonado — Phone: (909) 594-1405 ext. 33110 ### Immunization Requirements Before any child under age 18 is admitted to any pre-kindergarten facility or (TK/K-12) school in California, public or private, California law (17 CCR section 6065) requires a parent or guardian to present an immunization record. The record must: identify the student by name & date of birth; show the month, date, and year each required vaccine was received; have the type of vaccine; and include the name of the physician or agency who gave the vaccine. (Reference: California Immunization Handbook 2019, pg. 5.) Resources: www.ShotsForSchool.org ### Medication During School Hours California Education Code Section 49423 allows the school nurse or other designated school personnel to assist students who must take medication during the school day. If a physician/dentist visit may result in prescribed medication or a dosage change, stop by the school office or download the Medication Required During School Consent. ### Health Office Forms Allergy Questionnaire; Asthm

What are the key details about IB Diploma Requirements?

**Page title:** IB Diploma Requirements To earn an International Baccalaureate diploma, students must: **Complete courses in six academic subjects, one from each of the IB six subject groups:** - IB diploma candidates must select one subject from each of the six subject groups; - a second subject from groups 3 or 4 may be substituted for a subject in group 6; - three IB subjects must be taken at the higher level (HL), and three at the standard level (SL). A student is also permitted to take 4 HL and 2 SL courses. - A student is only permitted to complete a maximum of two SL courses during junior year. **Complete the core elements:** - the extended essay, a personal research paper; - the Theory of Knowledge, a senior seminar course designed to connect all of the disciplines; - CAS, a student action requirement requiring extra-curricular work in creativity, activity, and service. IB students must meet 7 learning outcomes with their CAS projects. All of the above must be done during students' junior and senior years. **Academic standards:** - Freshmen and sophomores: All students in DBHS IB prep must maintain a 3.0 or higher cumulative GPA in the Groups 1–5 academic courses — reviewed

What are the most important points about Transcripts?

**Page title:** Transcripts Current and past students access official transcripts through Docufide by Parchment, which enables users to instantly send transcripts to institutions of choice. To register accurately through Parchment, the following is required: use ONLY ONE email address per student; register using your LEGAL NAME; current residence address; date of birth; year of graduation. Contact **Mrs. Han** if your request is not processed after 2 business days. Using Docufide enables users to request transcripts for personal use and to send to universities, scholarship foundations, and/or the NCAA Eligibility Center. **Special Note for Current Seniors:** Final transcripts are requested by the student using Parchment. Seniors must follow the steps to request their FINAL transcript to be sent to the university they will attend. This is not done directly by DBHS or the GLC — it will only be sent if the student has requested the final transcript through Parchment. - Register & order transcripts: www.parchment.com - Direct link: http://www.parchment.com/c/college/high-school-2725-Diamond-Bar-High-School.html ---

How does Wellness Center work?

**Page title:** Wellness Center **DBHS Wellness Center — "Empowering Student Wellness from Within"** ### Mission Statement The Diamond Bar High School Wellness Staff is committed to creating a supportive environment for students by providing wellness education and resources. Peer counselors are equipped to assist students by guiding them to develop healthy strategies for personal well-being, which promotes individual academic success and improves the overall school climate. ### Vision Statement The Wellness Staff continually strives to be responsive to the overall needs of all students. They believe the physical, emotional, social, intellectual, cultural, and spiritual dimensions of students are the foundation of a well-balanced approach to personal success. The Wellness Center is an inclusive, nonjudgmental space available for all students seeking additional support and/or access to wellness services, education, and resources. **Location:** Room #254. Opened January 2016. Drop by during periods 1–4. Related items: Welcome to Wellness; Wellness Center Calendar; "Students @ Thrive: A Wellness Forum"; therapy dog "Daisy" visits Mondays and Thursdays.

What procedures are associated with Awards & Recognition?

**Page title:** Awards — U.S. News & World Report's Best High Schools U.S. News & World Reports - Best High Schools: recognized 2008–2024 (every year listed 2008 through 2024). US News & World Report published their high school rankings for 2024-25 on Tuesday, April 23. Once again, DBHS was ranked among the best schools in the state and the nation. The U.S. News Best High Schools rankings include data on nearly 25,000 public high schools in 50 states and the District of Columbia. Nearly 18,000 schools were ranked on six factors based on their performance on state assessments and how well they prepare students for college. **Rankings (2024-25):** - #408 in National Rankings - #56 in California High Schools - #21 in Los Angeles, CA Metro Area High Schools - #149 in STEM High Schools DBHS's rankings are a result of a partnership among students, staff, parents, community members, and the district office in supporting the balance provided by the four A's: Academics, Activities, the Arts, and Athletics. At the center of these foundations is the belief in providing equitable access to opportunities for success to ALL students. **The six ranking factors:** | Factor | Weight | Description |
