What information is available regarding Vision, Mission & SLOs?

**Page title:** Vision, Mission & SLOs ### Vision Statement Diamond Bar High School will be a premier school committed to the intellectual, physical, and social development of every student. Diamond Bar will be a community where every student's cultural heritage enriches the learning experience for all students. ### Mission Diamond Bar will value the five "A's" of Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics so that each student will experience a personalized educational program selected from a rigorous, rich and varied curriculum. Students will be challenged to discover their unique gifts and talents; staff members will support every student in realizing his or her potential. Working in partnership with parents and the community, the Diamond Bar High School staff will provide learning opportunities that reach beyond the boundaries of the classroom and ensure every student a chance for success in college and career, and as citizens and leaders of the 21st century. Related links: WASC; SARC; Vision, Mission & SLOs; SLO poster (PDF).

Guest, MSN, RN, NCSN — Phone: 951-453-3316, Fax: 909-895-8301 - **Health Clerk:** Evelyn Maldonado — Phone: (909) 594-1405 ext. 33110 ### Immunization Requirements Before any child under age 18 is admitted to any pre-kindergarten facility or (TK/K-12) school in California, public or private, California law (17 CCR section 6065) requires a parent or guardian to present an immunization record. The record must: identify the student by name & date of birth; show the month, date, and year each required vaccine was received; have the type of vaccine; and include the name of the physician or agency who gave the vaccine. (Reference: California Immunization Handbook 2019, pg. 5.) Resources: www.ShotsForSchool.org ### Medication During School Hours California Education Code Section 49423 allows the school nurse or other designated school personnel to assist students who must take medication during the school day. If a physician/dentist visit may result in prescribed medication or a dosage change, stop by the school office or download the Medication Required During School Consent. ### Health Office Forms Allergy Questionnaire; Asthm

How is Diamond related to History & Community?

**Page title:** School Info Diamond Bar High School (DBHS) opened in 1982 and is located in the city of Diamond Bar, California. The southern portion of the city is served by the Walnut Valley Unified School District, while the northern portion is served by the Pomona Unified School District. DBHS is one of two comprehensive high schools in the Walnut Valley Unified School District. The district also offers a continuation high school, three middle schools and nine elementary schools. Diamond Bar is a multi-ethnic, upper middle-class bedroom community stretching along the 57 and 60 freeways on the eastern edge of Los Angeles County. In 2021, the population of Diamond Bar was 53,857, the median income was $100,730, and in 2017, the median age was 38 years. The city is served by two different school districts, Pomona Unified on the north side and Walnut Valley Unified on the southern side. Diamond Bar is also an affluent and aging community. Since 2014, the segment of the population who ethnically identifies as white decreased from 30% to 21.7% and the Asian/Pacific Islander population has remained stable at 53.1%. What resources does Student Fees offer?

**Page title:** Student Fees (Fee Info) The California Constitution mandates that public education be provided to students free of charge, unless a charge is specifically authorized by law for a particular program or activity. This constitutional right of free access encompasses all educational activities, whether curricular or extracurricular, and regardless of whether credit is awarded for the educational activity. The right of free access also prohibits mandated purchases of materials, supplies, equipment or uniforms associated with the activity, as well as the payment of security deposits for access, participation, materials or equipment. A process that allows for a waiver process for an otherwise mandatory fee, charge or deposit does not render it constitutionally permissible. Further information (district): http://www.wvusd.k12.ca.us/apps/pages/index.jsp?uREC_ID=298183&type=d
What services are provided by Transcripts?

**Page title:** Transcripts Current and past students access official transcripts through Docufide by Parchment, which enables users to instantly send transcripts to institutions of choice. To register accurately through Parchment, the following is required: use ONLY ONE email address per student; register using your LEGAL NAME; current residence address; date of birth; year of graduation. Contact **Mrs. Han** if your request is not processed after 2 business days. Using Docufide enables users to request transcripts for personal use and to send to universities, scholarship foundations, and/or the NCAA Eligibility Center. **Special Note for Current Seniors:** Final transcripts are requested by the student using Parchment. Seniors must follow the steps to request their FINAL transcript to be sent to the university they will attend. This is not done directly by DBHS or the GLC — it will only be sent if the student has requested the final transcript through Parchment. - Register & order transcripts: www.parchment.com - Direct link: http://www.parchment.com/c/college/high-school-2725-Diamond-Bar-High-School.html ---

**Page title:** About USB / Activities (USB) **Motto:** "Raising the Bar" United Student Body (USB) is the student government at Diamond Bar High School. USB seeks to improve campus life and help students. USB is responsible for many on-campus activities and programs including lunchtime activities, rallies, dances, Student Store, and clubs approvals/budgets. The term USB was chosen over ASB during the 1982 student body convention. USB has 6 executive board members and 34 commissioners. The term of office is from January to December. Members are enrolled in 4th period Leadership class. **How to Join:** Executive board (e-board) candidates are first approved by the director, **Mrs. (Janna) Van Horn**, and then elected. Summarize International Baccalaureate (IB).

**Page title:** IB Program The International Baccalaureate (IB) aims to develop inquiring, knowledgeable and caring young people who help to create a better and more peaceful world through intercultural understanding and respect. The IB has four programs for students aged 3-19. These programs encourage students across the world to become active, compassionate and lifelong learners. They empower students to take ownership in their own learning and help them develop the knowledge, skills, and sense of purpose they need to thrive throughout their lives. Diamond Bar High School offers the IB Diploma Program (DP). The IBDP is a challenging two-year curriculum, primarily aimed at students aged 16 to 19. It leads to a qualification widely recognized by the world's leading universities. The program prepares students for university and encourages them to: ask challenging questions; learn how to learn; develop a strong sense of their own identity and culture; and develop the ability to communicate with and understand people from other countries and cultures. ### Donations The DBHS IB program accepts donations to support IB student activities, social events, and classroom supplies. **Page title:** Grade Level Coordinators (Guidance home) A GLC is a Grade Level Coordinator. Each grade level has two GLCs that assist each student. Grade Level Coordinators act as parents on campus. Explain Vision, Mission & SLOs in detail.

**Page title:** Vision, Mission & SLOs ### Vision Statement Diamond Bar High School will be a premier school committed to the intellectual, physical, and social development of every student. Diamond Bar will be a community where every student's cultural heritage enriches the learning experience for all students. ### Mission Diamond Bar will value the five "A's" of Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics so that each student will experience a personalized educational program selected from a rigorous, rich and varied curriculum. Students will be challenged to discover their unique gifts and talents; staff members will support every student in realizing his or her potential. Working in partnership with parents and the community, the Diamond Bar High School staff will provide learning opportunities that reach beyond the boundaries of the classroom and ensure every student a chance for success in college and career, and as citizens and leaders of the 21st century. Related links: WASC; SARC; Vision, Mission & SLOs; SLO poster (PDF).
How is listed related to Awards & Recognition?

**Page title:** Awards — U.S. News & World Report's Best High Schools U.S. News & World Reports - Best High Schools: recognized 2008–2024 (every year listed 2008 through 2024). US News & World Report published their high school rankings for 2024-25 on Tuesday, April 23. Once again, DBHS was ranked among the best schools in the state and the nation. The U.S. News Best High Schools rankings include data on nearly 25,000 public high schools in 50 states and the District of Columbia. Nearly 18,000 schools were ranked on six factors based on their performance on state assessments and how well they prepare students for college. **Rankings (2024-25):** - #408 in National Rankings - #56 in California High Schools - #21 in Los Angeles, CA Metro Area High Schools - #149 in STEM High Schools DBHS's rankings are a result of a partnership among students, staff, parents, community members, and the district office in supporting the balance provided by the four A's: Academics, Activities, the Arts, and Athletics. At the center of these foundations is the belief in providing equitable access to opportunities for success to ALL students. **The six ranking factors:** | Factor | Weight | Description |

How is International related to International Baccalaureate (IB)?

**Page title:** IB Program The International Baccalaureate (IB) aims to develop inquiring, knowledgeable and caring young people who help to create a better and more peaceful world through intercultural understanding and respect. The IB has four programs for students aged 3-19. These programs encourage students across the world to become active, compassionate and lifelong learners. They empower students to take ownership in their own learning and help them develop the knowledge, skills, and sense of purpose they need to thrive throughout their lives. Diamond Bar High School offers the IB Diploma Program (DP). The IBDP is a challenging two-year curriculum, primarily aimed at students aged 16 to 19. It leads to a qualification widely recognized by the world's leading universities. The program prepares students for university and encourages them to: ask challenging questions; learn how to learn; develop a strong sense of their own identity and culture; and develop the ability to communicate with and understand people from other countries and cultures. ### Donations The DBHS IB program accepts donations to support IB student activities, social events, and classroom supplies. How can a student use Work Permits?

**Page title:** Work Permits Students under the age of eighteen (18) must have a work permit on file with the school and their employer. IT'S THE LAW! ### To apply for a work permit: 1. Once you have been hired by an employer: - Complete the Minor's Information section - Complete the School Information Section - Have the application signed by the minor's parent/legal guardian - Have the employer complete and sign the Employer Information section. 2. To turn in a completed work permit, go see **Ms. Ayala** in the Career Center, located inside the Express Library (Rm. 265). Processing a work permit can take 1–2 business days. ### Work Permit Renewals If you are under the age of 18, work permits must be renewed each year. Follow the instructions above to renew. ### Young Workers Health and Safety Information Visit http://www.youngworkers.org/ for the basics about young worker health and safety, with a downloadable fact sheet "Are You A Working Teen?" in English and Spanish. Linked documents: DBHS Work Permit (PDF); CA Child Labor Laws (https://www.dir.ca.gov/dlse/childlaborlawpamphlet.pdf).

Methods: - **Email** — include student's full name, student ID number, grade level, date(s) of absence, and reason. - **Note** — sent with the student; include full name, ID number, grade, date(s), reason, and parent signature. - **Call the attendance office** at the clerk extensions above. **Voicemail:** If you reach voicemail, leave: student's full name, student ID number, date of absence/tardy/early leave, and specific reason. (To excuse a student early, refer to early-leave procedures.) Failure to provide this information may result in incorrect recording. **Incorrectly marked absent:** Students should monitor attendance via Aeries regularly or obtain a printout from the Attendance Office. Why is Attendance Office important?

**Page title:** Attendance Office **Absence line:** (909) 594-1860. **Attendance Clerks:** - **Amber Luna** — Attendance Clerk, students A–Lin — (909) 594-1405 ext. 33124 - **Jennifer Chung** — Attendance Clerk, students Lio–Z — (909) 594-1405 ext. 33123 ### Attendance FAQ **How to report an absence/tardy:** Absences must be cleared within 3 days; tardies/late arrivals within 1 day. Methods: - **Email** — include student's full name, student ID number, grade level, date(s) of absence, and reason. - **Note** — sent with the student; include full name, ID number, grade, date(s), reason, and parent signature. - **Call the attendance office** at the clerk extensions above. **Voicemail:** If you reach voicemail, leave: student's full name, student ID number, date of absence/tardy/early leave, and specific reason. (To excuse a student early, refer to early-leave procedures.) Failure to provide this information may result in incorrect recording. **Incorrectly marked absent:** Students should monitor attendance via Aeries regularly or obtain a printout from the Attendance Office. How is About related to Activities & United Student Body (USB)?

**Page title:** About USB / Activities (USB) **Motto:** "Raising the Bar" United Student Body (USB) is the student government at Diamond Bar High School. USB seeks to improve campus life and help students. USB is responsible for many on-campus activities and programs including lunchtime activities, rallies, dances, Student Store, and clubs approvals/budgets. The term USB was chosen over ASB during the 1982 student body convention. USB has 6 executive board members and 34 commissioners. The term of office is from January to December. Members are enrolled in 4th period Leadership class. **How to Join:** Executive board (e-board) candidates are first approved by the director, **Mrs. (Janna) Van Horn**, and then elected. Commissioners are selected by the executive board through a paper application and two interviews. ### USB 2025 Members **Activities Director:** Janna Van Horn **Executive Board** | Position | Member | | --- | --- | | USB President | Miles Lai | | Vice President | Daniel Lee | | Speaker of the House | Matthew Giang | | Finance Director | Michelle Park | | IOC | Noah Gonzales | | Secretary | Alena Mangsat | **Class Presidents (as printed)** | Position | Member | | --- |

How does Care Solace / Crisis Resources work?

**Page title:** Care Solace Resource document: Care Solace Family Letter (PDF). - **LA County ACCESS / Hotline:** 1-800-854-7771 — operates 24 hours/day, 7 days/week as the entry point for mental health services in Los Angeles County. Services include deployment of crisis evaluation teams, information and referrals, gatekeeping of acute inpatient psychiatric beds, interpreter services and patient transport. In a disaster, the Hotline provides screening, assessment, referral, and crisis counseling over the telephone. - **Crisis Text Line:** Text HOME to 741741 — https://www.crisistextline.org/

How is intellectual related to Vision, Mission & SLOs?

**Page title:** Vision, Mission & SLOs ### Vision Statement Diamond Bar High School will be a premier school committed to the intellectual, physical, and social development of every student. Diamond Bar will be a community where every student's cultural heritage enriches the learning experience for all students. ### Mission Diamond Bar will value the five "A's" of Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics so that each student will experience a personalized educational program selected from a rigorous, rich and varied curriculum. Students will be challenged to discover their unique gifts and talents; staff members will support every student in realizing his or her potential. Working in partnership with parents and the community, the Diamond Bar High School staff will provide learning opportunities that reach beyond the boundaries of the classroom and ensure every student a chance for success in college and career, and as citizens and leaders of the 21st century. Related links: WASC; SARC; Vision, Mission & SLOs; SLO poster (PDF).
What should a new student know about Health Office?

**Page title:** Health Office - **School Nurse:** Theresa A. Guest, MSN, RN, NCSN — Phone: 951-453-3316, Fax: 909-895-8301 - **Health Clerk:** Evelyn Maldonado — Phone: (909) 594-1405 ext. 33110 ### Immunization Requirements Before any child under age 18 is admitted to any pre-kindergarten facility or (TK/K-12) school in California, public or private, California law (17 CCR section 6065) requires a parent or guardian to present an immunization record. The record must: identify the student by name & date of birth; show the month, date, and year each required vaccine was received; have the type of vaccine; and include the name of the physician or agency who gave the vaccine. (Reference: California Immunization Handbook 2019, pg. 5.) Resources: www.ShotsForSchool.org ### Medication During School Hours California Education Code Section 49423 allows the school nurse or other designated school personnel to assist students who must take medication during the school day. If a physician/dentist visit may result in prescribed medication or a dosage change, stop by the school office or download the Medication Required During School Consent. ### Health Office Forms Allergy Questionnaire; Asthm
What procedures are associated with History & Community?

**Page title:** School Info Diamond Bar High School (DBHS) opened in 1982 and is located in the city of Diamond Bar, California. The southern portion of the city is served by the Walnut Valley Unified School District, while the northern portion is served by the Pomona Unified School District. DBHS is one of two comprehensive high schools in the Walnut Valley Unified School District. The district also offers a continuation high school, three middle schools and nine elementary schools. Diamond Bar is a multi-ethnic, upper middle-class bedroom community stretching along the 57 and 60 freeways on the eastern edge of Los Angeles County. In 2021, the population of Diamond Bar was 53,857, the median income was $100,730, and in 2017, the median age was 38 years. The city is served by two different school districts, Pomona Unified on the north side and Walnut Valley Unified on the southern side. Diamond Bar is also an affluent and aging community. Since 2014, the segment of the population who ethnically identifies as white decreased from 30% to 21.7% and the Asian/Pacific Islander population has remained stable at 53.1%. Give me a detailed overview of History & Community.

**Page title:** School Info Diamond Bar High School (DBHS) opened in 1982 and is located in the city of Diamond Bar, California. The southern portion of the city is served by the Walnut Valley Unified School District, while the northern portion is served by the Pomona Unified School District. DBHS is one of two comprehensive high schools in the Walnut Valley Unified School District. The district also offers a continuation high school, three middle schools and nine elementary schools. Diamond Bar is a multi-ethnic, upper middle-class bedroom community stretching along the 57 and 60 freeways on the eastern edge of Los Angeles County. In 2021, the population of Diamond Bar was 53,857, the median income was $100,730, and in 2017, the median age was 38 years. The city is served by two different school districts, Pomona Unified on the north side and Walnut Valley Unified on the southern side. Diamond Bar is also an affluent and aging community. Since 2014, the segment of the population who ethnically identifies as white decreased from 30% to 21.7% and the Asian/Pacific Islander population has remained stable at 53.1%. Compared to the average community in the State of California, Diamond

The record must: identify the student by name & date of birth; show the month, date, and year each required vaccine was received; have the type of vaccine; and include the name of the physician or agency who gave the vaccine. (Reference: California Immunization Handbook 2019, pg. 5.) Resources: www.ShotsForSchool.org ### Medication During School Hours California Education Code Section 49423 allows the school nurse or other designated school personnel to assist students who must take medication during the school day. How is Brahma related to Brahma Tech Academy (PLTW / Engineering)?

**Page title:** Brahma Tech Academy – Welcome Apply at brahma-tech.com/apply (deadline referenced: Thursday, February 26, 2026). More info: brahma-tech.com. **Program Overview:** Brahma Tech Academy students cultivate their STEM identity, develop expertise, and are empowered to showcase their STEM leadership. Students distinguish themselves through concentrated engagement in STEM courses and extracurriculars, culminating in a 150-hour internship in their chosen field. Brahma Tech graduates are qualified for entry into 4-year universities and possess technical and professional skills in demand by industry leaders. The curriculum spans four years. 9th grade consists of an entry-level course in (1) Innovations in Technology, (2) Intro to Engineering Design (PLTW), or (3) AP Computer Science Principles. In grades 10–12, students take courses in 7 different strands. The program concludes with a 150-hour internship. Students who complete the program receive a Brahma Tech STEM certificate and wear the academy stole at graduation. Coordinators: **Barbara Pentangelo** and **Jeremiah Wai**. (The "7 Strands" course list is hosted in an external Canva document.)

How is Student related to Wellness Center?

**Page title:** Wellness Center **DBHS Wellness Center — "Empowering Student Wellness from Within"** ### Mission Statement The Diamond Bar High School Wellness Staff is committed to creating a supportive environment for students by providing wellness education and resources. Peer counselors are equipped to assist students by guiding them to develop healthy strategies for personal well-being, which promotes individual academic success and improves the overall school climate. ### Vision Statement The Wellness Staff continually strives to be responsive to the overall needs of all students. They believe the physical, emotional, social, intellectual, cultural, and spiritual dimensions of students are the foundation of a well-balanced approach to personal success. The Wellness Center is an inclusive, nonjudgmental space available for all students seeking additional support and/or access to wellness services, education, and resources. **Location:** Room #254. Opened January 2016. Drop by during periods 1–4. Related items: Welcome to Wellness; Wellness Center Calendar; "Students @ Thrive: A Wellness Forum"; therapy dog "Daisy" visits Mondays and Thursdays.

Explain Work Permits in detail.

**Page title:** Work Permits Students under the age of eighteen (18) must have a work permit on file with the school and their employer. IT'S THE LAW! ### To apply for a work permit: 1. Once you have been hired by an employer: - Complete the Minor's Information section - Complete the School Information Section - Have the application signed by the minor's parent/legal guardian - Have the employer complete and sign the Employer Information section. 2. To turn in a completed work permit, go see **Ms. Ayala** in the Career Center, located inside the Express Library (Rm. 265). Processing a work permit can take 1–2 business days. ### Work Permit Renewals If you are under the age of 18, work permits must be renewed each year. Follow the instructions above to renew. ### Young Workers Health and Safety Information Visit http://www.youngworkers.org/ for the basics about young worker health and safety, with a downloadable fact sheet "Are You A Working Teen?" in English and Spanish. (Mt. SAC sponsored classes run 7:30 AM – 1:30 PM.) ### Three Summer School Programs **I. WVUSD Non-Tuition Remediation of D's/F's — Graduation/CSU/UC or Enrichment** WVUSD non-tuition classes are for students who need to retake a course for high school graduation credits. What services are provided by History & Community?

**Page title:** School Info Diamond Bar High School (DBHS) opened in 1982 and is located in the city of Diamond Bar, California. The southern portion of the city is served by the Walnut Valley Unified School District, while the northern portion is served by the Pomona Unified School District. DBHS is one of two comprehensive high schools in the Walnut Valley Unified School District. The district also offers a continuation high school, three middle schools and nine elementary schools. Diamond Bar is a multi-ethnic, upper middle-class bedroom community stretching along the 57 and 60 freeways on the eastern edge of Los Angeles County. In 2021, the population of Diamond Bar was 53,857, the median income was $100,730, and in 2017, the median age was 38 years. The city is served by two different school districts, Pomona Unified on the north side and Walnut Valley Unified on the southern side. Diamond Bar is also an affluent and aging community. Since 2014, the segment of the population who ethnically identifies as white decreased from 30% to 21.7% and the Asian/Pacific Islander population has remained stable at 53.1%. Summarize Brahma Tech Academy (PLTW / Engineering).

**Page title:** Brahma Tech Academy – Welcome Apply at brahma-tech.com/apply (deadline referenced: Thursday, February 26, 2026). More info: brahma-tech.com. **Program Overview:** Brahma Tech Academy students cultivate their STEM identity, develop expertise, and are empowered to showcase their STEM leadership. Students distinguish themselves through concentrated engagement in STEM courses and extracurriculars, culminating in a 150-hour internship in their chosen field. Brahma Tech graduates are qualified for entry into 4-year universities and possess technical and professional skills in demand by industry leaders. The curriculum spans four years. 9th grade consists of an entry-level course in (1) Innovations in Technology, (2) Intro to Engineering Design (PLTW), or (3) AP Computer Science Principles. In grades 10–12, students take courses in 7 different strands. The program concludes with a 150-hour internship. Students who complete the program receive a Brahma Tech STEM certificate and wear the academy stole at graduation. Coordinators: **Barbara Pentangelo** and **Jeremiah Wai**. (The "7 Strands" course list is hosted in an external Canva document.)

Give me a detailed overview of IB Diploma Requirements.

**Page title:** IB Diploma Requirements To earn an International Baccalaureate diploma, students must: **Complete courses in six academic subjects, one from each of the IB six subject groups:** - IB diploma candidates must select one subject from each of the six subject groups; - a second subject from groups 3 or 4 may be substituted for a subject in group 6; - three IB subjects must be taken at the higher level (HL), and three at the standard level (SL). A student is also permitted to take 4 HL and 2 SL courses. - A student is only permitted to complete a maximum of two SL courses during junior year. **Complete the core elements:** - the extended essay, a personal research paper; - the Theory of Knowledge, a senior seminar course designed to connect all of the disciplines; - CAS, a student action requirement requiring extra-curricular work in creativity, activity, and service. IB students must meet 7 learning outcomes with their CAS projects. All of the above must be done during students' junior and senior years. **Academic standards:** - Freshmen and sophomores: All students in DBHS IB prep must maintain a 3.0 or higher cumulative GPA in the Groups 1–5 academic courses — reviewed

How is students related to IB Diploma Requirements?

**Page title:** IB Diploma Requirements To earn an International Baccalaureate diploma, students must: **Complete courses in six academic subjects, one from each of the IB six subject groups:** - IB diploma candidates must select one subject from each of the six subject groups; - a second subject from groups 3 or 4 may be substituted for a subject in group 6; - three IB subjects must be taken at the higher level (HL), and three at the standard level (SL). A student is also permitted to take 4 HL and 2 SL courses. - A student is only permitted to complete a maximum of two SL courses during junior year. **Complete the core elements:** - the extended essay, a personal research paper; - the Theory of Knowledge, a senior seminar course designed to connect all of the disciplines; - CAS, a student action requirement requiring extra-curricular work in creativity, activity, and service. IB students must meet 7 learning outcomes with their CAS projects. All of the above must be done during students' junior and senior years. **Academic standards:** - Freshmen and sophomores: All students in DBHS IB prep must maintain a 3.0 or higher cumulative GPA in the Groups 1–5 academic courses — reviewed

Guest, MSN, RN, NCSN — Phone: 951-453-3316, Fax: 909-895-8301 - **Health Clerk:** Evelyn Maldonado — Phone: (909) 594-1405 ext. 33110 ### Immunization Requirements Before any child under age 18 is admitted to any pre-kindergarten facility or (TK/K-12) school in California, public or private, California law (17 CCR section 6065) requires a parent or guardian to present an immunization record. The record must: identify the student by name & date of birth; show the month, date, and year each required vaccine was received; have the type of vaccine; and include the name of the physician or agency who gave the vaccine. (Reference: California Immunization Handbook 2019, pg. 5.) Resources: www.ShotsForSchool.org ### Medication During School Hours California Education Code Section 49423 allows the school nurse or other designated school personnel to assist students who must take medication during the school day. If a physician/dentist visit may result in prescribed medication or a dosage change, stop by the school office or download the Medication Required During School Consent. ### Health Office Forms Allergy Questionnaire; Asthm

What is Wellness Center?

**Page title:** Wellness Center **DBHS Wellness Center — "Empowering Student Wellness from Within"** ### Mission Statement The Diamond Bar High School Wellness Staff is committed to creating a supportive environment for students by providing wellness education and resources. Peer counselors are equipped to assist students by guiding them to develop healthy strategies for personal well-being, which promotes individual academic success and improves the overall school climate. ### Vision Statement The Wellness Staff continually strives to be responsive to the overall needs of all students. They believe the physical, emotional, social, intellectual, cultural, and spiritual dimensions of students are the foundation of a well-balanced approach to personal success. The Wellness Center is an inclusive, nonjudgmental space available for all students seeking additional support and/or access to wellness services, education, and resources. **Location:** Room #254. Opened January 2016. Drop by during periods 1–4. Related items: Welcome to Wellness; Wellness Center Calendar; "Students @ Thrive: A Wellness Forum"; therapy dog "Daisy" visits Mondays and Thursdays.

How can a student use International Baccalaureate (IB)?

**Page title:** IB Program The International Baccalaureate (IB) aims to develop inquiring, knowledgeable and caring young people who help to create a better and more peaceful world through intercultural understanding and respect. The IB has four programs for students aged 3-19. These programs encourage students across the world to become active, compassionate and lifelong learners. They empower students to take ownership in their own learning and help them develop the knowledge, skills, and sense of purpose they need to thrive throughout their lives. Diamond Bar High School offers the IB Diploma Program (DP). The IBDP is a challenging two-year curriculum, primarily aimed at students aged 16 to 19. It leads to a qualification widely recognized by the world's leading universities. The program prepares students for university and encourages them to: ask challenging questions; learn how to learn; develop a strong sense of their own identity and culture; and develop the ability to communicate with and understand people from other countries and cultures. ### Donations The DBHS IB program accepts donations to support IB student activities, social events, and classroom supplies. How is Mission related to Vision, Mission & SLOs?

**Page title:** Vision, Mission & SLOs ### Vision Statement Diamond Bar High School will be a premier school committed to the intellectual, physical, and social development of every student. Diamond Bar will be a community where every student's cultural heritage enriches the learning experience for all students. ### Mission Diamond Bar will value the five "A's" of Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics so that each student will experience a personalized educational program selected from a rigorous, rich and varied curriculum. Students will be challenged to discover their unique gifts and talents; staff members will support every student in realizing his or her potential. Working in partnership with parents and the community, the Diamond Bar High School staff will provide learning opportunities that reach beyond the boundaries of the classroom and ensure every student a chance for success in college and career, and as citizens and leaders of the 21st century. Give me a detailed overview of Transcripts.

**Page title:** Transcripts Current and past students access official transcripts through Docufide by Parchment, which enables users to instantly send transcripts to institutions of choice. To register accurately through Parchment, the following is required: use ONLY ONE email address per student; register using your LEGAL NAME; current residence address; date of birth; year of graduation. Contact **Mrs. Han** if your request is not processed after 2 business days. Using Docufide enables users to request transcripts for personal use and to send to universities, scholarship foundations, and/or the NCAA Eligibility Center. **Special Note for Current Seniors:** Final transcripts are requested by the student using Parchment. Seniors must follow the steps to request their FINAL transcript to be sent to the university they will attend. This is not done directly by DBHS or the GLC — it will only be sent if the student has requested the final transcript through Parchment. - Register & order transcripts: www.parchment.com - Direct link: http://www.parchment.com/c/college/high-school-2725-Diamond-Bar-High-School.html ---

How can a student use History & Community?

**Page title:** School Info Diamond Bar High School (DBHS) opened in 1982 and is located in the city of Diamond Bar, California. The southern portion of the city is served by the Walnut Valley Unified School District, while the northern portion is served by the Pomona Unified School District. DBHS is one of two comprehensive high schools in the Walnut Valley Unified School District. The district also offers a continuation high school, three middle schools and nine elementary schools. Diamond Bar is a multi-ethnic, upper middle-class bedroom community stretching along the 57 and 60 freeways on the eastern edge of Los Angeles County. In 2021, the population of Diamond Bar was 53,857, the median income was $100,730, and in 2017, the median age was 38 years. The city is served by two different school districts, Pomona Unified on the north side and Walnut Valley Unified on the southern side. Diamond Bar is also an affluent and aging community. Since 2014, the segment of the population who ethnically identifies as white decreased from 30% to 21.7% and the Asian/Pacific Islander population has remained stable at 53.1%. What are the key details about Vision, Mission & SLOs?

**Page title:** Vision, Mission & SLOs ### Vision Statement Diamond Bar High School will be a premier school committed to the intellectual, physical, and social development of every student. Diamond Bar will be a community where every student's cultural heritage enriches the learning experience for all students. ### Mission Diamond Bar will value the five "A's" of Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics so that each student will experience a personalized educational program selected from a rigorous, rich and varied curriculum. Students will be challenged to discover their unique gifts and talents; staff members will support every student in realizing his or her potential. Working in partnership with parents and the community, the Diamond Bar High School staff will provide learning opportunities that reach beyond the boundaries of the classroom and ensure every student a chance for success in college and career, and as citizens and leaders of the 21st century. Guest, MSN, RN, NCSN — Phone: 951-453-3316, Fax: 909-895-8301 - **Health Clerk:** Evelyn Maldonado — Phone: (909) 594-1405 ext. 33110 ### Immunization Requirements Before any child under age 18 is admitted to any pre-kindergarten facility or (TK/K-12) school in California, public or private, California law (17 CCR section 6065) requires a parent or guardian to present an immunization record. The record must: identify the student by name & date of birth; show the month, date, and year each required vaccine was received; have the type of vaccine; and include the name of the physician or agency who gave the vaccine. (Reference: California Immunization Handbook 2019, pg. 5.) Resources: www.ShotsForSchool.org ### Medication During School Hours California Education Code Section 49423 allows the school nurse or other designated school personnel to assist students who must take medication during the school day. If a physician/dentist visit may result in prescribed medication or a dosage change, stop by the school office or download the Medication Required During School Consent. ### Health Office Forms Allergy Questionnaire; Asthm

Why is Work Permits important?

**Page title:** Work Permits Students under the age of eighteen (18) must have a work permit on file with the school and their employer. IT'S THE LAW! ### To apply for a work permit: 1. Once you have been hired by an employer: - Complete the Minor's Information section - Complete the School Information Section - Have the application signed by the minor's parent/legal guardian - Have the employer complete and sign the Employer Information section. 2. To turn in a completed work permit, go see **Ms. Ayala** in the Career Center, located inside the Express Library (Rm. 265). Processing a work permit can take 1–2 business days. ### Work Permit Renewals If you are under the age of 18, work permits must be renewed each year. Follow the instructions above to renew. ### Young Workers Health and Safety Information Visit http://www.youngworkers.org/ for the basics about young worker health and safety, with a downloadable fact sheet "Are You A Working Teen?" in English and Spanish. Linked documents: DBHS Work Permit (PDF); CA Child Labor Laws (https://www.dir.ca.gov/dlse/childlaborlawpamphlet.pdf).

How is premier related to Vision, Mission & SLOs?

**Page title:** Vision, Mission & SLOs ### Vision Statement Diamond Bar High School will be a premier school committed to the intellectual, physical, and social development of every student. Diamond Bar will be a community where every student's cultural heritage enriches the learning experience for all students. ### Mission Diamond Bar will value the five "A's" of Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics so that each student will experience a personalized educational program selected from a rigorous, rich and varied curriculum. Students will be challenged to discover their unique gifts and talents; staff members will support every student in realizing his or her potential. Working in partnership with parents and the community, the Diamond Bar High School staff will provide learning opportunities that reach beyond the boundaries of the classroom and ensure every student a chance for success in college and career, and as citizens and leaders of the 21st century. Related links: WASC; SARC; Vision, Mission & SLOs; SLO poster (PDF).

What procedures are associated with IB Diploma Requirements?

**Page title:** IB Diploma Requirements To earn an International Baccalaureate diploma, students must: **Complete courses in six academic subjects, one from each of the IB six subject groups:** - IB diploma candidates must select one subject from each of the six subject groups; - a second subject from groups 3 or 4 may be substituted for a subject in group 6; - three IB subjects must be taken at the higher level (HL), and three at the standard level (SL). A student is also permitted to take 4 HL and 2 SL courses. - A student is only permitted to complete a maximum of two SL courses during junior year. **Complete the core elements:** - the extended essay, a personal research paper; - the Theory of Knowledge, a senior seminar course designed to connect all of the disciplines; - CAS, a student action requirement requiring extra-curricular work in creativity, activity, and service. IB students must meet 7 learning outcomes with their CAS projects. All of the above must be done during students' junior and senior years. **Academic standards:** - Freshmen and sophomores: All students in DBHS IB prep must maintain a 3.0 or higher cumulative GPA in the Groups 1–5 academic courses — reviewed

**Date:** May 26, 2026 **Source URL:** https://dbhs.wvusd.org/apps/news/article/2202825?categoryId=1430 Congratulations to DBHS and CTE alum Abby Kottke (CO 2022) on graduation from CSULB and the presentation of their documentary films at the Full Spectrum 2026 Cinematic Showcase at the Carpenter Performing Arts Center. Ms. Kottke is a recent graduate of the CSULB Cinematic Arts program. At DBHS she was a CTE pathway completer of the Digital Video Production program and the Brahma Cinematics Club President. She received a $500 scholarship in collaboration with the non-profit REACH for Mental Health for her Eye Opener documentary, *Going Off Script Olivia James*. (The Eye Opener category encourages students to see filmmaking as a form of social justice and awareness of social issues.) Her films: - **ALIGNMENT** — "An observation of a bonsai workshop and the various challenges students face." Director: Abby Kottke; Cinematographer: Kehan Liu. - **RIICHI REACH** — "An observational portrait of the Kenko Mahjong Tournament at LAPOM, a LA Riichi Mahjong Club in Gardena, CA." Provide a complete summary of Wellness Center.

**Page title:** Wellness Center **DBHS Wellness Center — "Empowering Student Wellness from Within"** ### Mission Statement The Diamond Bar High School Wellness Staff is committed to creating a supportive environment for students by providing wellness education and resources. Peer counselors are equipped to assist students by guiding them to develop healthy strategies for personal well-being, which promotes individual academic success and improves the overall school climate. ### Vision Statement The Wellness Staff continually strives to be responsive to the overall needs of all students. They believe the physical, emotional, social, intellectual, cultural, and spiritual dimensions of students are the foundation of a well-balanced approach to personal success. The Wellness Center is an inclusive, nonjudgmental space available for all students seeking additional support and/or access to wellness services, education, and resources. **Location:** Room #254. Opened January 2016. Drop by during periods 1–4. Related items: Welcome to Wellness; Wellness Center Calendar; "Students @ Thrive: A Wellness Forum"; therapy dog "Daisy" visits Mondays and Thursdays.

How is social related to Vision, Mission & SLOs?

**Page title:** Vision, Mission & SLOs ### Vision Statement Diamond Bar High School will be a premier school committed to the intellectual, physical, and social development of every student. Diamond Bar will be a community where every student's cultural heritage enriches the learning experience for all students. ### Mission Diamond Bar will value the five "A's" of Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics so that each student will experience a personalized educational program selected from a rigorous, rich and varied curriculum. Students will be challenged to discover their unique gifts and talents; staff members will support every student in realizing his or her potential. Working in partnership with parents and the community, the Diamond Bar High School staff will provide learning opportunities that reach beyond the boundaries of the classroom and ensure every student a chance for success in college and career, and as citizens and leaders of the 21st century. Related links: WASC; SARC; Vision, Mission & SLOs; SLO poster (PDF).

How can a student use Student Fees?

**Page title:** Student Fees (Fee Info) The California Constitution mandates that public education be provided to students free of charge, unless a charge is specifically authorized by law for a particular program or activity. This constitutional right of free access encompasses all educational activities, whether curricular or extracurricular, and regardless of whether credit is awarded for the educational activity. The right of free access also prohibits mandated purchases of materials, supplies, equipment or uniforms associated with the activity, as well as the payment of security deposits for access, participation, materials or equipment. A process that allows for a waiver process for an otherwise mandatory fee, charge or deposit does not render it constitutionally permissible. Further information (district): http://www.wvusd.k12.ca.us/apps/pages/index.jsp?uREC_ID=298183&type=d

Explain Activities & United Student Body (USB) in detail.

**Page title:** About USB / Activities (USB) **Motto:** "Raising the Bar" United Student Body (USB) is the student government at Diamond Bar High School. USB seeks to improve campus life and help students. USB is responsible for many on-campus activities and programs including lunchtime activities, rallies, dances, Student Store, and clubs approvals/budgets. The term USB was chosen over ASB during the 1982 student body convention. USB has 6 executive board members and 34 commissioners. The term of office is from January to December. Members are enrolled in 4th period Leadership class. **How to Join:** Executive board (e-board) candidates are first approved by the director, **Mrs. (Janna) Van Horn**, and then elected. Commissioners are selected by the executive board through a paper application and two interviews. ### USB 2025 Members **Activities Director:** Janna Van Horn **Executive Board** | Position | Member | | --- | --- | | USB President | Miles Lai | | Vice President | Daniel Lee | | Speaker of the House | Matthew Giang | | Finance Director | Michelle Park | | IOC | Noah Gonzales | | Secretary | Alena Mangsat | **Class Presidents (as printed)** | Position | Member | | --- |

Tell me about Work Permits.

**Page title:** Work Permits Students under the age of eighteen (18) must have a work permit on file with the school and their employer. IT'S THE LAW! ### To apply for a work permit: 1. Once you have been hired by an employer: - Complete the Minor's Information section - Complete the School Information Section - Have the application signed by the minor's parent/legal guardian - Have the employer complete and sign the Employer Information section. 2. To turn in a completed work permit, go see **Ms. Ayala** in the Career Center, located inside the Express Library (Rm. 265). Processing a work permit can take 1–2 business days. ### Work Permit Renewals If you are under the age of 18, work permits must be renewed each year. Follow the instructions above to renew. ### Young Workers Health and Safety Information Visit http://www.youngworkers.org/ for the basics about young worker health and safety, with a downloadable fact sheet "Are You A Working Teen?" in English and Spanish. Provide information about Transcripts.

**Page title:** Transcripts Current and past students access official transcripts through Docufide by Parchment, which enables users to instantly send transcripts to institutions of choice. To register accurately through Parchment, the following is required: use ONLY ONE email address per student; register using your LEGAL NAME; current residence address; date of birth; year of graduation. Contact **Mrs. Han** if your request is not processed after 2 business days. Using Docufide enables users to request transcripts for personal use and to send to universities, scholarship foundations, and/or the NCAA Eligibility Center. **Special Note for Current Seniors:** Final transcripts are requested by the student using Parchment. Seniors must follow the steps to request their FINAL transcript to be sent to the university they will attend. This is not done directly by DBHS or the GLC — it will only be sent if the student has requested the final transcript through Parchment. - Register & order transcripts: www.parchment.com - Direct link: http://www.parchment.com/c/college/high-school-2725-Diamond-Bar-High-School.html ---
How is Program related to Brahma Tech Academy (PLTW / Engineering)?

**Page title:** Brahma Tech Academy – Welcome Apply at brahma-tech.com/apply (deadline referenced: Thursday, February 26, 2026). More info: brahma-tech.com. **Program Overview:** Brahma Tech Academy students cultivate their STEM identity, develop expertise, and are empowered to showcase their STEM leadership. Students distinguish themselves through concentrated engagement in STEM courses and extracurriculars, culminating in a 150-hour internship in their chosen field. Brahma Tech graduates are qualified for entry into 4-year universities and possess technical and professional skills in demand by industry leaders. The curriculum spans four years. 9th grade consists of an entry-level course in (1) Innovations in Technology, (2) Intro to Engineering Design (PLTW), or (3) AP Computer Science Principles. In grades 10–12, students take courses in 7 different strands. The program concludes with a 150-hour internship. Students who complete the program receive a Brahma Tech STEM certificate and wear the academy stole at graduation. Coordinators: **Barbara Pentangelo** and **Jeremiah Wai**. (The "7 Strands" course list is hosted in an external Canva document.)

What are the key details about DBHS AND CTE - Alumni Spotlight - Abby Kottke?

**Date:** May 26, 2026 **Source URL:** https://dbhs.wvusd.org/apps/news/article/2202825?categoryId=1430 Congratulations to DBHS and CTE alum Abby Kottke (CO 2022) on graduation from CSULB and the presentation of their documentary films at the Full Spectrum 2026 Cinematic Showcase at the Carpenter Performing Arts Center. Ms. Kottke is a recent graduate of the CSULB Cinematic Arts program. At DBHS she was a CTE pathway completer of the Digital Video Production program and the Brahma Cinematics Club President. She received a $500 scholarship in collaboration with the non-profit REACH for Mental Health for her Eye Opener documentary, *Going Off Script Olivia James*. (The Eye Opener category encourages students to see filmmaking as a form of social justice and awareness of social issues.) Her films: - **ALIGNMENT** — "An observation of a bonsai workshop and the various challenges students face." Director: Abby Kottke; Cinematographer: Kehan Liu. - **RIICHI REACH** — "An observational portrait of the Kenko Mahjong Tournament at LAPOM, a LA Riichi Mahjong Club in Gardena, CA." Provide a complete summary of DVP and PCA Students Attend Directing Change Awards Ceremony.

What are the requirements related to Vision, Mission & SLOs?

**Page title:** Vision, Mission & SLOs ### Vision Statement Diamond Bar High School will be a premier school committed to the intellectual, physical, and social development of every student. Diamond Bar will be a community where every student's cultural heritage enriches the learning experience for all students. ### Mission Diamond Bar will value the five "A's" of Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics so that each student will experience a personalized educational program selected from a rigorous, rich and varied curriculum. Students will be challenged to discover their unique gifts and talents; staff members will support every student in realizing his or her potential. Working in partnership with parents and the community, the Diamond Bar High School staff will provide learning opportunities that reach beyond the boundaries of the classroom and ensure every student a chance for success in college and career, and as citizens and leaders of the 21st century. USB seeks to improve campus life and help students. How is Baccalaureate related to IB Diploma Requirements?

**Page title:** IB Diploma Requirements To earn an International Baccalaureate diploma, students must: **Complete courses in six academic subjects, one from each of the IB six subject groups:** - IB diploma candidates must select one subject from each of the six subject groups; - a second subject from groups 3 or 4 may be substituted for a subject in group 6; - three IB subjects must be taken at the higher level (HL), and three at the standard level (SL). A student is also permitted to take 4 HL and 2 SL courses. - A student is only permitted to complete a maximum of two SL courses during junior year. **Complete the core elements:** - the extended essay, a personal research paper; - the Theory of Knowledge, a senior seminar course designed to connect all of the disciplines; - CAS, a student action requirement requiring extra-curricular work in creativity, activity, and service. IB students must meet 7 learning outcomes with their CAS projects. All of the above must be done during students' junior and senior years. **Academic standards:** - Freshmen and sophomores: All students in DBHS IB prep must maintain a 3.0 or higher cumulative GPA in the Groups 1–5 academic courses — reviewed

What should a new student know about History & Community?

**Page title:** School Info Diamond Bar High School (DBHS) opened in 1982 and is located in the city of Diamond Bar, California. The southern portion of the city is served by the Walnut Valley Unified School District, while the northern portion is served by the Pomona Unified School District. DBHS is one of two comprehensive high schools in the Walnut Valley Unified School District. The district also offers a continuation high school, three middle schools and nine elementary schools. Diamond Bar is a multi-ethnic, upper middle-class bedroom community stretching along the 57 and 60 freeways on the eastern edge of Los Angeles County. In 2021, the population of Diamond Bar was 53,857, the median income was $100,730, and in 2017, the median age was 38 years. The city is served by two different school districts, Pomona Unified on the north side and Walnut Valley Unified on the southern side. Diamond Bar is also an affluent and aging community. Since 2014, the segment of the population who ethnically identifies as white decreased from 30% to 21.7% and the Asian/Pacific Islander population has remained stable at 53.1%. **Page title:** Health Office - **School Nurse:** Theresa A. Guest, MSN, RN, NCSN — Phone: 951-453-3316, Fax: 909-895-8301 - **Health Clerk:** Evelyn Maldonado — Phone: (909) 594-1405 ext. 33110 ### Immunization Requirements Before any child under age 18 is admitted to any pre-kindergarten facility or (TK/K-12) school in California, public or private, California law (17 CCR section 6065) requires a parent or guardian to present an immunization record. The record must: identify the student by name & date of birth; show the month, date, and year each required vaccine was received; have the type of vaccine; and include the name of the physician or agency who gave the vaccine. (Reference: California Immunization Handbook 2019, pg. 5.) Resources: www.ShotsForSchool.org ### Medication During School Hours California Education Code Section 49423 allows the school nurse or other designated school personnel to assist students who must take medication during the school day. If a physician/dentist visit may result in prescribed medication or a dosage change, stop by the school office or download the Medication Required During School Consent. ### Health Office Forms Allergy Questionnaire; Asthm

What are the requirements related to Activities & United Student Body (USB)?

**Page title:** About USB / Activities (USB) **Motto:** "Raising the Bar" United Student Body (USB) is the student government at Diamond Bar High School. USB seeks to improve campus life and help students. USB is responsible for many on-campus activities and programs including lunchtime activities, rallies, dances, Student Store, and clubs approvals/budgets. The term USB was chosen over ASB during the 1982 student body convention. USB has 6 executive board members and 34 commissioners. The term of office is from January to December. Members are enrolled in 4th period Leadership class. **How to Join:** Executive board (e-board) candidates are first approved by the director, **Mrs. (Janna) Van Horn**, and then elected. Commissioners are selected by the executive board through a paper application and two interviews. ### USB 2025 Members **Activities Director:** Janna Van Horn **Executive Board** | Position | Member | | --- | --- | | USB President | Miles Lai | | Vice President | Daniel Lee | | Speaker of the House | Matthew Giang | | Finance Director | Michelle Park | | IOC | Noah Gonzales | | Secretary | Alena Mangsat | **Class Presidents (as printed)** | Position | Member | | --- |

How is apply related to Work Permits?

**Page title:** Work Permits Students under the age of eighteen (18) must have a work permit on file with the school and their employer. IT'S THE LAW! ### To apply for a work permit: 1. Once you have been hired by an employer: - Complete the Minor's Information section - Complete the School Information Section - Have the application signed by the minor's parent/legal guardian - Have the employer complete and sign the Employer Information section. 2. To turn in a completed work permit, go see **Ms. Ayala** in the Career Center, located inside the Express Library (Rm. 265). Processing a work permit can take 1–2 business days. ### Work Permit Renewals If you are under the age of 18, work permits must be renewed each year. Follow the instructions above to renew. ### Young Workers Health and Safety Information Visit http://www.youngworkers.org/ for the basics about young worker health and safety, with a downloadable fact sheet "Are You A Working Teen?" in English and Spanish. Linked documents: DBHS Work Permit (PDF); CA Child Labor Laws (https://www.dir.ca.gov/dlse/childlaborlawpamphlet.pdf).

How is users related to Transcripts?

**Page title:** Transcripts Current and past students access official transcripts through Docufide by Parchment, which enables users to instantly send transcripts to institutions of choice. To register accurately through Parchment, the following is required: use ONLY ONE email address per student; register using your LEGAL NAME; current residence address; date of birth; year of graduation. Contact **Mrs. Han** if your request is not processed after 2 business days. Using Docufide enables users to request transcripts for personal use and to send to universities, scholarship foundations, and/or the NCAA Eligibility Center. **Special Note for Current Seniors:** Final transcripts are requested by the student using Parchment. Seniors must follow the steps to request their FINAL transcript to be sent to the university they will attend. This is not done directly by DBHS or the GLC — it will only be sent if the student has requested the final transcript through Parchment. - Register & order transcripts: www.parchment.com - Direct link: http://www.parchment.com/c/college/high-school-2725-Diamond-Bar-High-School.html ---

What is Activities & United Student Body (USB)?

**Page title:** About USB / Activities (USB) **Motto:** "Raising the Bar" United Student Body (USB) is the student government at Diamond Bar High School. USB seeks to improve campus life and help students. USB is responsible for many on-campus activities and programs including lunchtime activities, rallies, dances, Student Store, and clubs approvals/budgets. The term USB was chosen over ASB during the 1982 student body convention. USB has 6 executive board members and 34 commissioners. The term of office is from January to December. Members are enrolled in 4th period Leadership class. **How to Join:** Executive board (e-board) candidates are first approved by the director, **Mrs. (Janna) Van Horn**, and then elected. Commissioners are selected by the executive board through a paper application and two interviews. ### USB 2025 Members **Activities Director:** Janna Van Horn **Executive Board** | Position | Member | | --- | --- | | USB President | Miles Lai | | Vice President | Daniel Lee | | Speaker of the House | Matthew Giang | | Finance Director | Michelle Park | | IOC | Noah Gonzales | | Secretary | Alena Mangsat | **Class Presidents (as printed)** | Position | Member | | --- |

Give me a detailed overview of Brahma Tech Academy (PLTW / Engineering).

**Page title:** Brahma Tech Academy – Welcome Apply at brahma-tech.com/apply (deadline referenced: Thursday, February 26, 2026). More info: brahma-tech.com. **Program Overview:** Brahma Tech Academy students cultivate their STEM identity, develop expertise, and are empowered to showcase their STEM leadership. Students distinguish themselves through concentrated engagement in STEM courses and extracurriculars, culminating in a 150-hour internship in their chosen field. Brahma Tech graduates are qualified for entry into 4-year universities and possess technical and professional skills in demand by industry leaders. The curriculum spans four years. 9th grade consists of an entry-level course in (1) Innovations in Technology, (2) Intro to Engineering Design (PLTW), or (3) AP Computer Science Principles. In grades 10–12, students take courses in 7 different strands. The program concludes with a 150-hour internship. Students who complete the program receive a Brahma Tech STEM certificate and wear the academy stole at graduation. Coordinators: **Barbara Pentangelo** and **Jeremiah Wai**. (The "7 Strands" course list is hosted in an external Canva document.)

Methods: - **Email** — include student's full name, student ID number, grade level, date(s) of absence, and reason. - **Note** — sent with the student; include full name, ID number, grade, date(s), reason, and parent signature. - **Call the attendance office** at the clerk extensions above. **Voicemail:** If you reach voicemail, leave: student's full name, student ID number, date of absence/tardy/early leave, and specific reason. (To excuse a student early, refer to early-leave procedures.) Failure to provide this information may result in incorrect recording. **Incorrectly marked absent:** Students should monitor attendance via Aeries regularly or obtain a printout from the Attendance Office. How is Unified related to History & Community?

**Page title:** School Info Diamond Bar High School (DBHS) opened in 1982 and is located in the city of Diamond Bar, California. The southern portion of the city is served by the Walnut Valley Unified School District, while the northern portion is served by the Pomona Unified School District. DBHS is one of two comprehensive high schools in the Walnut Valley Unified School District. The district also offers a continuation high school, three middle schools and nine elementary schools. Diamond Bar is a multi-ethnic, upper middle-class bedroom community stretching along the 57 and 60 freeways on the eastern edge of Los Angeles County. In 2021, the population of Diamond Bar was 53,857, the median income was $100,730, and in 2017, the median age was 38 years. The city is served by two different school districts, Pomona Unified on the north side and Walnut Valley Unified on the southern side. Diamond Bar is also an affluent and aging community. Since 2014, the segment of the population who ethnically identifies as white decreased from 30% to 21.7% and the Asian/Pacific Islander population has remained stable at 53.1%. **Page title:** Grade Level Coordinators (Guidance home) A GLC is a Grade Level Coordinator. Each grade level has two GLCs that assist each student. Grade Level Coordinators act as parents on campus. They handle conflicts that may occur with any of the students, they can assist with what classes to take and what college admission policies are, and they can give guidance to any student looking for someone to talk to. Tell me about Activities & United Student Body (USB).

**Page title:** About USB / Activities (USB) **Motto:** "Raising the Bar" United Student Body (USB) is the student government at Diamond Bar High School. USB seeks to improve campus life and help students. USB is responsible for many on-campus activities and programs including lunchtime activities, rallies, dances, Student Store, and clubs approvals/budgets. The term USB was chosen over ASB during the 1982 student body convention. USB has 6 executive board members and 34 commissioners. The term of office is from January to December. Members are enrolled in 4th period Leadership class. **How to Join:** Executive board (e-board) candidates are first approved by the director, **Mrs. (Janna) Van Horn**, and then elected. Commissioners are selected by the executive board through a paper application and two interviews. ### USB 2025 Members **Activities Director:** Janna Van Horn **Executive Board** | Position | Member | | --- | --- | | USB President | Miles Lai | | Vice President | Daniel Lee | | Speaker of the House | Matthew Giang | | Finance Director | Michelle Park | | IOC | Noah Gonzales | | Secretary | Alena Mangsat | **Class Presidents (as printed)** | Position | Member | | --- |

Can you explain Awards & Recognition?

**Page title:** Awards — U.S. News & World Report's Best High Schools U.S. News & World Reports - Best High Schools: recognized 2008–2024 (every year listed 2008 through 2024). US News & World Report published their high school rankings for 2024-25 on Tuesday, April 23. Once again, DBHS was ranked among the best schools in the state and the nation. The U.S. News Best High Schools rankings include data on nearly 25,000 public high schools in 50 states and the District of Columbia. Nearly 18,000 schools were ranked on six factors based on their performance on state assessments and how well they prepare students for college. **Rankings (2024-25):** - #408 in National Rankings - #56 in California High Schools - #21 in Los Angeles, CA Metro Area High Schools - #149 in STEM High Schools DBHS's rankings are a result of a partnership among students, staff, parents, community members, and the district office in supporting the balance provided by the four A's: Academics, Activities, the Arts, and Athletics. At the center of these foundations is the belief in providing equitable access to opportunities for success to ALL students. **The six ranking factors:** | Factor | Weight | Description |

How is section related to Work Permits?

**Page title:** Work Permits Students under the age of eighteen (18) must have a work permit on file with the school and their employer. IT'S THE LAW! ### To apply for a work permit: 1. Once you have been hired by an employer: - Complete the Minor's Information section - Complete the School Information Section - Have the application signed by the minor's parent/legal guardian - Have the employer complete and sign the Employer Information section. 2. To turn in a completed work permit, go see **Ms. Ayala** in the Career Center, located inside the Express Library (Rm. 265). Processing a work permit can take 1–2 business days. ### Work Permit Renewals If you are under the age of 18, work permits must be renewed each year. Follow the instructions above to renew. ### Young Workers Health and Safety Information Visit http://www.youngworkers.org/ for the basics about young worker health and safety, with a downloadable fact sheet "Are You A Working Teen?" in English and Spanish. What does the DBHS knowledge base say about Vision, Mission & SLOs?

**Page title:** Vision, Mission & SLOs ### Vision Statement Diamond Bar High School will be a premier school committed to the intellectual, physical, and social development of every student. Diamond Bar will be a community where every student's cultural heritage enriches the learning experience for all students. ### Mission Diamond Bar will value the five "A's" of Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics so that each student will experience a personalized educational program selected from a rigorous, rich and varied curriculum. Students will be challenged to discover their unique gifts and talents; staff members will support every student in realizing his or her potential. Working in partnership with parents and the community, the Diamond Bar High School staff will provide learning opportunities that reach beyond the boundaries of the classroom and ensure every student a chance for success in college and career, and as citizens and leaders of the 21st century. Related links: WASC; SARC; Vision, Mission & SLOs; SLO poster (PDF).

What information is available regarding Student Fees?

**Page title:** Student Fees (Fee Info) The California Constitution mandates that public education be provided to students free of charge, unless a charge is specifically authorized by law for a particular program or activity. This constitutional right of free access encompasses all educational activities, whether curricular or extracurricular, and regardless of whether credit is awarded for the educational activity. The right of free access also prohibits mandated purchases of materials, supplies, equipment or uniforms associated with the activity, as well as the payment of security deposits for access, participation, materials or equipment. A process that allows for a waiver process for an otherwise mandatory fee, charge or deposit does not render it constitutionally permissible. Further information (district): http://www.wvusd.k12.ca.us/apps/pages/index.jsp?uREC_ID=298183&type=d

Guest, MSN, RN, NCSN — Phone: 951-453-3316, Fax: 909-895-8301 - **Health Clerk:** Evelyn Maldonado — Phone: (909) 594-1405 ext. 33110 ### Immunization Requirements Before any child under age 18 is admitted to any pre-kindergarten facility or (TK/K-12) school in California, public or private, California law (17 CCR section 6065) requires a parent or guardian to present an immunization record. The record must: identify the student by name & date of birth; show the month, date, and year each required vaccine was received; have the type of vaccine; and include the name of the physician or agency who gave the vaccine. (Reference: California Immunization Handbook 2019, pg. 5.) Resources: www.ShotsForSchool.org ### Medication During School Hours California Education Code Section 49423 allows the school nurse or other designated school personnel to assist students who must take medication during the school day. If a physician/dentist visit may result in prescribed medication or a dosage change, stop by the school office or download the Medication Required During School Consent. ### Health Office Forms Allergy Questionnaire; Asthm
How does IB Diploma Requirements work?

**Page title:** IB Diploma Requirements To earn an International Baccalaureate diploma, students must: **Complete courses in six academic subjects, one from each of the IB six subject groups:** - IB diploma candidates must select one subject from each of the six subject groups; - a second subject from groups 3 or 4 may be substituted for a subject in group 6; - three IB subjects must be taken at the higher level (HL), and three at the standard level (SL). A student is also permitted to take 4 HL and 2 SL courses. - A student is only permitted to complete a maximum of two SL courses during junior year. **Complete the core elements:** - the extended essay, a personal research paper; - the Theory of Knowledge, a senior seminar course designed to connect all of the disciplines; - CAS, a student action requirement requiring extra-curricular work in creativity, activity, and service. IB students must meet 7 learning outcomes with their CAS projects. All of the above must be done during students' junior and senior years. **Academic standards:** - Freshmen and sophomores: All students in DBHS IB prep must maintain a 3.0 or higher cumulative GPA in the Groups 1–5 academic courses — reviewed

How is seeks related to Activities & United Student Body (USB)?

**Page title:** About USB / Activities (USB) **Motto:** "Raising the Bar" United Student Body (USB) is the student government at Diamond Bar High School. USB seeks to improve campus life and help students. USB is responsible for many on-campus activities and programs including lunchtime activities, rallies, dances, Student Store, and clubs approvals/budgets. The term USB was chosen over ASB during the 1982 student body convention. USB has 6 executive board members and 34 commissioners. The term of office is from January to December. Members are enrolled in 4th period Leadership class. **How to Join:** Executive board (e-board) candidates are first approved by the director, **Mrs. (Janna) Van Horn**, and then elected. Commissioners are selected by the executive board through a paper application and two interviews. ### USB 2025 Members **Activities Director:** Janna Van Horn **Executive Board** | Position | Member | | --- | --- | | USB President | Miles Lai | | Vice President | Daniel Lee | | Speaker of the House | Matthew Giang | | Finance Director | Michelle Park | | IOC | Noah Gonzales | | Secretary | Alena Mangsat | **Class Presidents (as printed)** | Position | Member | | --- |
Provide a complete summary of Brahma Tech Academy (PLTW / Engineering).

**Page title:** Brahma Tech Academy – Welcome Apply at brahma-tech.com/apply (deadline referenced: Thursday, February 26, 2026). More info: brahma-tech.com. **Program Overview:** Brahma Tech Academy students cultivate their STEM identity, develop expertise, and are empowered to showcase their STEM leadership. Students distinguish themselves through concentrated engagement in STEM courses and extracurriculars, culminating in a 150-hour internship in their chosen field. Brahma Tech graduates are qualified for entry into 4-year universities and possess technical and professional skills in demand by industry leaders. The curriculum spans four years. 9th grade consists of an entry-level course in (1) Innovations in Technology, (2) Intro to Engineering Design (PLTW), or (3) AP Computer Science Principles. In grades 10–12, students take courses in 7 different strands. The program concludes with a 150-hour internship. Students who complete the program receive a Brahma Tech STEM certificate and wear the academy stole at graduation. Coordinators: **Barbara Pentangelo** and **Jeremiah Wai**. (The "7 Strands" course list is hosted in an external Canva document.)

What procedures are associated with International Baccalaureate (IB)?

**Page title:** IB Program The International Baccalaureate (IB) aims to develop inquiring, knowledgeable and caring young people who help to create a better and more peaceful world through intercultural understanding and respect. The IB has four programs for students aged 3-19. These programs encourage students across the world to become active, compassionate and lifelong learners. They empower students to take ownership in their own learning and help them develop the knowledge, skills, and sense of purpose they need to thrive throughout their lives. Diamond Bar High School offers the IB Diploma Program (DP). The IBDP is a challenging two-year curriculum, primarily aimed at students aged 16 to 19. It leads to a qualification widely recognized by the world's leading universities. The program prepares students for university and encourages them to: ask challenging questions; learn how to learn; develop a strong sense of their own identity and culture; and develop the ability to communicate with and understand people from other countries and cultures. ### Donations The DBHS IB program accepts donations to support IB student activities, social events, and classroom supplies. **Page title:** DBHS Mathematics! Welcome to Brahma Mathematics. - 2011, 2013, 2014, 2016, 2022 Regional Champions for the California Mathematics League - 2005 and 2007 AP Calculus World Champions - Winner of the prestigious 2007-2008 Siemens Award for Advanced Placement Any student who wants a recommendation letter from a math teacher for a summer off-campus opportunity or scholarship must submit a request to that teacher no later than THREE WEEKS before the application due date. What are the requirements related to Health Office?

**Page title:** Health Office - **School Nurse:** Theresa A. Guest, MSN, RN, NCSN — Phone: 951-453-3316, Fax: 909-895-8301 - **Health Clerk:** Evelyn Maldonado — Phone: (909) 594-1405 ext. 33110 ### Immunization Requirements Before any child under age 18 is admitted to any pre-kindergarten facility or (TK/K-12) school in California, public or private, California law (17 CCR section 6065) requires a parent or guardian to present an immunization record. The record must: identify the student by name & date of birth; show the month, date, and year each required vaccine was received; have the type of vaccine; and include the name of the physician or agency who gave the vaccine. (Reference: California Immunization Handbook 2019, pg. 5.) Resources: www.ShotsForSchool.org ### Medication During School Hours California Education Code Section 49423 allows the school nurse or other designated school personnel to assist students who must take medication during the school day. If a physician/dentist visit may result in prescribed medication or a dosage change, stop by the school office or download the Medication Required During School Consent. ### Health Office Forms Allergy Questionnaire; Asthm

If you used your school email or a nickname, contact College Board to change it.
