(Do not pay for an exam for a class you are not enrolled in.) ### Important AP Dates (as published) - September 27, 2024: Last day to turn in AP Fee Reduction Forms (Room 557) - November 6, 2024: Last day to pay for your AP Exam (yearlong and Fall semester classes). After this, a $40 late fee to pay and a $40 cancellation fee. - March 5, 2025: Last day to pay for Spring-only semester classes; last day to cancel an exam with a $40 cancellation fee (no refund after this day); last day to order an exam with a $40 late order fee. What are the most important points about Grade Level Coordinators?

**Page title:** Grade Level Coordinators (Guidance home) A GLC is a Grade Level Coordinator. Each grade level has two GLCs that assist each student. Grade Level Coordinators act as parents on campus. They handle conflicts that may occur with any of the students, they can assist with what classes to take and what college admission policies are, and they can give guidance to any student looking for someone to talk to. Grade Level Coordinators are out on campus during Brunch and Lunch and invite students to contact them during this time for any assistance needed. ### Grade Level Coordinator Assignments | Group | Coordinators | | --- | --- | | Pathways Academy | Ms. Brummett | | Class of 2026 | Mr. Desmond and Mrs. Tumbeiro | | Class of 2027 | Mr. Gonzales and Mrs. Burns | | Class of 2028 | Ms. Lee and Mrs. Zimmerman | | Class of 2029 | Mr. Galeener and Mrs. Duenas | | Class of 2030 | Mr. Desmond and Ms. Brummett | **School Code:** To sign up for the SAT, use the College Board website. Methods: - **Email** — include student's full name, student ID number, grade level, date(s) of absence, and reason. - **Note** — sent with the student; include full name, ID number, grade, date(s), reason, and parent signature. - **Call the attendance office** at the clerk extensions above. **Voicemail:** If you reach voicemail, leave: student's full name, student ID number, date of absence/tardy/early leave, and specific reason. (To excuse a student early, refer to early-leave procedures.) Failure to provide this information may result in incorrect recording. **Incorrectly marked absent:** Students should monitor attendance via Aeries regularly or obtain a printout from the Attendance Office. Provide a complete summary of Work Permits.

**Page title:** Work Permits Students under the age of eighteen (18) must have a work permit on file with the school and their employer. IT'S THE LAW! ### To apply for a work permit: 1. Once you have been hired by an employer: - Complete the Minor's Information section - Complete the School Information Section - Have the application signed by the minor's parent/legal guardian - Have the employer complete and sign the Employer Information section. 2. To turn in a completed work permit, go see **Ms. Ayala** in the Career Center, located inside the Express Library (Rm. 265). Processing a work permit can take 1–2 business days. ### Work Permit Renewals If you are under the age of 18, work permits must be renewed each year. Follow the instructions above to renew. ### Young Workers Health and Safety Information Visit http://www.youngworkers.org/ for the basics about young worker health and safety, with a downloadable fact sheet "Are You A Working Teen?" in English and Spanish. Linked documents: DBHS Work Permit (PDF); CA Child Labor Laws (https://www.dir.ca.gov/dlse/childlaborlawpamphlet.pdf).
What does the DBHS knowledge base say about Grade Level Coordinators?

**Page title:** Grade Level Coordinators (Guidance home) A GLC is a Grade Level Coordinator. Each grade level has two GLCs that assist each student. Grade Level Coordinators act as parents on campus. They handle conflicts that may occur with any of the students, they can assist with what classes to take and what college admission policies are, and they can give guidance to any student looking for someone to talk to. Grade Level Coordinators are out on campus during Brunch and Lunch and invite students to contact them during this time for any assistance needed. ### Grade Level Coordinator Assignments | Group | Coordinators | | --- | --- | | Pathways Academy | Ms. Brummett | | Class of 2026 | Mr. Desmond and Mrs. Tumbeiro | | Class of 2027 | Mr. Gonzales and Mrs. Burns | | Class of 2028 | Ms. Lee and Mrs. Zimmerman | | Class of 2029 | Mr. Galeener and Mrs. Duenas | | Class of 2030 | Mr. Desmond and Ms. Brummett | **School Code:** To sign up for the SAT, use the College Board website. Why is Awards & Recognition important?

**Page title:** Awards — U.S. News & World Report's Best High Schools U.S. News & World Reports - Best High Schools: recognized 2008–2024 (every year listed 2008 through 2024). US News & World Report published their high school rankings for 2024-25 on Tuesday, April 23. Once again, DBHS was ranked among the best schools in the state and the nation. The U.S. News Best High Schools rankings include data on nearly 25,000 public high schools in 50 states and the District of Columbia. Nearly 18,000 schools were ranked on six factors based on their performance on state assessments and how well they prepare students for college. **Rankings (2024-25):** - #408 in National Rankings - #56 in California High Schools - #21 in Los Angeles, CA Metro Area High Schools - #149 in STEM High Schools DBHS's rankings are a result of a partnership among students, staff, parents, community members, and the district office in supporting the balance provided by the four A's: Academics, Activities, the Arts, and Athletics. At the center of these foundations is the belief in providing equitable access to opportunities for success to ALL students. **The six ranking factors:** | Factor | Weight | Description |

Guest, MSN, RN, NCSN — Phone: 951-453-3316, Fax: 909-895-8301 - **Health Clerk:** Evelyn Maldonado — Phone: (909) 594-1405 ext. 33110 ### Immunization Requirements Before any child under age 18 is admitted to any pre-kindergarten facility or (TK/K-12) school in California, public or private, California law (17 CCR section 6065) requires a parent or guardian to present an immunization record. The record must: identify the student by name & date of birth; show the month, date, and year each required vaccine was received; have the type of vaccine; and include the name of the physician or agency who gave the vaccine. (Reference: California Immunization Handbook 2019, pg. 5.) Resources: www.ShotsForSchool.org ### Medication During School Hours California Education Code Section 49423 allows the school nurse or other designated school personnel to assist students who must take medication during the school day. If a physician/dentist visit may result in prescribed medication or a dosage change, stop by the school office or download the Medication Required During School Consent. ### Health Office Forms Allergy Questionnaire; Asthm

Methods: - **Email** — include student's full name, student ID number, grade level, date(s) of absence, and reason. - **Note** — sent with the student; include full name, ID number, grade, date(s), reason, and parent signature. - **Call the attendance office** at the clerk extensions above. **Voicemail:** If you reach voicemail, leave: student's full name, student ID number, date of absence/tardy/early leave, and specific reason. (To excuse a student early, refer to early-leave procedures.) Failure to provide this information may result in incorrect recording. **Incorrectly marked absent:** Students should monitor attendance via Aeries regularly or obtain a printout from the Attendance Office. What are the key details about Student Fees?

**Page title:** Student Fees (Fee Info) The California Constitution mandates that public education be provided to students free of charge, unless a charge is specifically authorized by law for a particular program or activity. This constitutional right of free access encompasses all educational activities, whether curricular or extracurricular, and regardless of whether credit is awarded for the educational activity. The right of free access also prohibits mandated purchases of materials, supplies, equipment or uniforms associated with the activity, as well as the payment of security deposits for access, participation, materials or equipment. A process that allows for a waiver process for an otherwise mandatory fee, charge or deposit does not render it constitutionally permissible. Further information (district): http://www.wvusd.k12.ca.us/apps/pages/index.jsp?uREC_ID=298183&type=d

How is served related to History & Community?

**Page title:** School Info Diamond Bar High School (DBHS) opened in 1982 and is located in the city of Diamond Bar, California. The southern portion of the city is served by the Walnut Valley Unified School District, while the northern portion is served by the Pomona Unified School District. DBHS is one of two comprehensive high schools in the Walnut Valley Unified School District. The district also offers a continuation high school, three middle schools and nine elementary schools. Diamond Bar is a multi-ethnic, upper middle-class bedroom community stretching along the 57 and 60 freeways on the eastern edge of Los Angeles County. In 2021, the population of Diamond Bar was 53,857, the median income was $100,730, and in 2017, the median age was 38 years. The city is served by two different school districts, Pomona Unified on the north side and Walnut Valley Unified on the southern side. Diamond Bar is also an affluent and aging community. Since 2014, the segment of the population who ethnically identifies as white decreased from 30% to 21.7% and the Asian/Pacific Islander population has remained stable at 53.1%. How is Student related to Student Fees?

**Page title:** Student Fees (Fee Info) The California Constitution mandates that public education be provided to students free of charge, unless a charge is specifically authorized by law for a particular program or activity. This constitutional right of free access encompasses all educational activities, whether curricular or extracurricular, and regardless of whether credit is awarded for the educational activity. The right of free access also prohibits mandated purchases of materials, supplies, equipment or uniforms associated with the activity, as well as the payment of security deposits for access, participation, materials or equipment. A process that allows for a waiver process for an otherwise mandatory fee, charge or deposit does not render it constitutionally permissible. Further information (district): http://www.wvusd.k12.ca.us/apps/pages/index.jsp?uREC_ID=298183&type=d

Can you explain Transcripts?

**Page title:** Transcripts Current and past students access official transcripts through Docufide by Parchment, which enables users to instantly send transcripts to institutions of choice. To register accurately through Parchment, the following is required: use ONLY ONE email address per student; register using your LEGAL NAME; current residence address; date of birth; year of graduation. Contact **Mrs. Han** if your request is not processed after 2 business days. Using Docufide enables users to request transcripts for personal use and to send to universities, scholarship foundations, and/or the NCAA Eligibility Center. **Special Note for Current Seniors:** Final transcripts are requested by the student using Parchment. Seniors must follow the steps to request their FINAL transcript to be sent to the university they will attend. This is not done directly by DBHS or the GLC — it will only be sent if the student has requested the final transcript through Parchment. - Register & order transcripts: www.parchment.com - Direct link: http://www.parchment.com/c/college/high-school-2725-Diamond-Bar-High-School.html ---

What procedures are associated with Work Permits?

**Page title:** Work Permits Students under the age of eighteen (18) must have a work permit on file with the school and their employer. IT'S THE LAW! ### To apply for a work permit: 1. Once you have been hired by an employer: - Complete the Minor's Information section - Complete the School Information Section - Have the application signed by the minor's parent/legal guardian - Have the employer complete and sign the Employer Information section. 2. To turn in a completed work permit, go see **Ms. Ayala** in the Career Center, located inside the Express Library (Rm. 265). Processing a work permit can take 1–2 business days. ### Work Permit Renewals If you are under the age of 18, work permits must be renewed each year. Follow the instructions above to renew. ### Young Workers Health and Safety Information Visit http://www.youngworkers.org/ for the basics about young worker health and safety, with a downloadable fact sheet "Are You A Working Teen?" in English and Spanish. ### Immunization Requirements
Before any child under age 18 is admitted to any pre-kindergarten facility or (TK/K-12) school in California, public or private, California law (17 CCR section 6065) requires a parent or guardian to present an immunization record.

How is Baccalaureate related to International Baccalaureate (IB)?

**Page title:** IB Program The International Baccalaureate (IB) aims to develop inquiring, knowledgeable and caring young people who help to create a better and more peaceful world through intercultural understanding and respect. The IB has four programs for students aged 3-19. These programs encourage students across the world to become active, compassionate and lifelong learners. They empower students to take ownership in their own learning and help them develop the knowledge, skills, and sense of purpose they need to thrive throughout their lives. Diamond Bar High School offers the IB Diploma Program (DP). The IBDP is a challenging two-year curriculum, primarily aimed at students aged 16 to 19. It leads to a qualification widely recognized by the world's leading universities. The program prepares students for university and encourages them to: ask challenging questions; learn how to learn; develop a strong sense of their own identity and culture; and develop the ability to communicate with and understand people from other countries and cultures. ### Donations The DBHS IB program accepts donations to support IB student activities, social events, and classroom supplies.
